Apple's silicon empire was built via deep vertical integration, 8,000+ engineers, and 15 design centers, making it highly defensible.
Alternative foundries like Intel 18A and Samsung are emerging as viable options, raising strategic questions for the future.

paragraphs.source: In 2013, TSMC made a $10 billion bet on a single customer. Morris Chang committed to building 20nm capacity with uncertain economics on the promise that Apple would fill those fabs. “I bet the company, but I didn’t think I would lose,” Chang later said. He was right. Apple’s A8 chip launched in 2014, and TSMC never looked back.

inline_elements.text: I bet the company, but I didn’t think I would lose

Morris Chang committed to building 20nm capacity with uncertain economics on the promise that Apple would fill those fabs. “*I bet the company, but I didn’t think I would lose*,” Chang later said. He was right. Apple’s A8 chip launched in 2014, and TSMC never looked back.

pro.text: 2013 年，台积电（TSMC）将 100 亿美元押注于单一客户。张忠谋在经济效益尚不明朗的情况下，仅凭苹果会填满这些晶圆厂（fab）产能的承诺，便拍板建设 20 纳米产能。“我押上了整个公司，但我没想过会输，”张忠谋后来回忆道。他的确赢了。苹果的 A8 芯片于 2014 年发布，台积电自此一路高歌猛进。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s annual spend at TSMC grew from $2B in 2014 to $24B in 2025. That is 12x in 12 years. Apple went from 9% of TSMC revenue to 25% at its peak and settled to 20% in 2025. More striking is Apple’s dominance at node launches: consistently >50% since 20nm and in some cases near 100%. Apple effectively funded the yield learning curve for every major node transition since 20nm.

Apple effectively funded the yield learning curve for every major node transition since 20nm.

pro.text: 苹果在台积电的年度支出从 2014 年的 20 亿美元增长到 2025 年的 240 亿美元。12 年间实现了 12 倍的跃升。苹果占台积电营收的比例从 9% 攀升至巅峰期的 25%，并在 2025 年稳定在 20%。更惊人的是苹果在新制程节点发布初期的统治力：自 20 纳米以来，其份额始终保持在 50% 以上，部分情况下甚至接近 100%。可以说，自 20 纳米以来的每一次重大制程节点迭代，其良率学习曲线的成本实际上都由苹果买了单。

paragraphs.source: The foundry model is dominant today. IDMs cannot support process development and fab capex with what is effectively a single customer. But even foundries need a “first and best” customer with large demand and deep pockets to fund their continued advance. Apple has been that customer for the last decade at TSMC. The juggernaut partnership has propelled both companies to new heights, put competitors in the dust, and fueled the chipmaking industry.

But even foundries need a “first and best” customer with large demand and deep pockets to fund their continued advance. Apple has been that customer for the last decade at TSMC. The juggernaut partnership has propelled both companies to new heights, put competitors in the dust, and fueled the chipmaking industry.

The juggernaut partnership has propelled both companies to new heights, put competitors in the dust, and fueled the chipmaking industry.

pro.text: 如今，晶圆代工模式占据主导地位。IDM 仅靠实质上的单一客户，根本无法支撑制程研发和晶圆厂资本支出（capex）。但即便是晶圆代工厂，也需要一个需求庞大且财力雄厚的“首发且最优”客户，来为其持续的技术演进提供资金。过去十年间，苹果在台积电扮演的正是这一角色。这对巨头联盟将两家公司双双推向新高度，将竞争对手远远甩在身后，并为整个芯片制造行业注入了强劲动力。

paragraphs.source: This report traces the Apple-TSMC relationship from Intel’s 2010 rejection through five distinct phases, examining how Apple built its chip empire through acquisitions and 8,000+ engineers across 15 design centers. We analyze why competitors have failed to replicate this vertical integration, map Apple’s manufacturing footprint across Fab 18 and advanced packaging facilities, and assess the strategic questions facing both companies as Intel 18A and Samsung re-engagement become viable alternatives. We’ll also discuss the next phase of the relationship. It’s not just Apple at the leading edge anymore. All of this is quantified with specifics from our proprietary Foundry Industry Model and Apple Wafer Demand Model.

We analyze why competitors have failed to replicate this vertical integration, map Apple’s manufacturing footprint across Fab 18 and advanced packaging facilities, and assess the strategic questions facing both companies as Intel 18A and Samsung re-engagement become viable alternatives. We’ll also discuss the next phase of the relationship. It’s not just Apple at the leading edge anymore. All of this is quantified with specifics from our proprietary [Foundry Industry Model](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/) and [Apple Wafer Demand Model](https://semianalysis.com/institutional/apple-tsmc-foundry-model-webinar-recap-key-takeaways/).
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: This report traces the Apple-TSMC relationship from Intel’s 2010 rejection through five distinct phases, examining how Apple built its chip empire through acquisitions and 8,000+ engineers across 15 design centers. We analyze why competitors have failed to replicate this vertical integration, map Apple’s manufacturing footprint across Fab 18 and advanced packaging facilities, and assess the strategic questions facing both companies as Intel 18A and Samsung re-engagement become viable alternatives. We’ll also discuss the next phase of the relationship. It’s not just Apple at the leading edge anymore. All of this is quantified with specifics from our proprietary Foundry Industry Model and Apple Wafer Demand Model.

issues.description: 中英文之间缺少空格（“英特尔18A”），且对“英特尔”和“三星”等行业极度常见的品牌进行英文括注（Intel, Samsung）属于过度加注，略微打断了阅读的流畅感。
“AI 重塑晶圆代工厂（foundry）版图下的权力动态演变”作为标题或副标题略显生硬。英文的 "as" 表伴随状态，转化为“随着……而演变”会更符合中文语感。同时，foundry 括注在此处略显多余。

paragraphs.source: You can see the Apple effect in TSMC’s capex trajectory. Pre-Apple (2005-2009), annual spending averaged $2.4B with no anchor tenant to de-risk investment. From 2019-2022, TSMC deployed $98B, exceeding the cumulative spend of the prior 14 years. Apple’s manufacturing purchase obligations scaled from $8.7B in 2010 to $71B in 2022. TSMC-specific payments grew from near-zero in 2013 to $23B+ in 2025. For over a decade, Apple was the only company capable of pre-funding bleeding-edge capacity at scale. That changed with Nvidia’s AI-driven cash generation. Today, two companies can fund TSMC’s roadmap.

pro.text: 观察台积电的资本支出轨迹，“苹果效应”清晰可见。在“前苹果时代”（2005-2009 年），台积电年均支出仅为 24 亿美元，且缺乏锚定客户（Anchor Tenant）为巨额投资兜底。到了 2019 至 2022 年，台积电豪掷 980 亿美元，一举超越此前 14 年的支出总和。苹果的制造采购承诺从 2010 年的 87 亿美元一路飙升至 2022 年的 710 亿美元。其中，专门支付给台积电的款项从 2013 年的近乎为零，暴涨至 2025 年的超 230 亿美元。十多年来，苹果始终是唯一有实力大规模预付资金、包下尖端产能的企业。但随着英伟达（NVIDIA）凭借 AI 业务创造强劲现金流，这一局面已被打破。如今，已有两家巨头能够为台积电的技术路线图提供资金支持。

paragraphs.source: The platform shift has already happened. HPC revenue at TSMC grew from 36% in 2020 to 58% in 2025. Smartphone revenue shrunk to 29% from 46%. Our model shows Nvidia will consume more N3 wafers than Apple by 4Q27. Apple’s share of N2 drops to 48%, the first time in a decade Apple is not the dominant customer on a new node.

pro.text: 平台更迭已然发生。台积电的 HPC 营收占比从 2020 年的 36% 攀升至 2025 年的 58%。智能手机营收占比则从 46% 萎缩至 29%。我们的模型显示，到 2027 年第四季度，英伟达消耗的 N3 晶圆数量将超越苹果。苹果在 N2 上的份额将降至 48%，这是十年来苹果首次失去新制程节点绝对主导客户的地位。

paragraphs.source: TSMC’s A16 is architected for HPC. Backside power delivery, gate-all-around transistors, and thermal envelopes are optimized for HPC and smartphones will skip this node. Nvidia benefits disproportionately. Apple’s N2 (N2 + A16) share drops not because Apple lost leverage, but because the node was built for a different customer. A14 (1.4nm) rebalances the equation. TSMC is designing A14 for both mobile and HPC from the start, with a separate backside power (TSMC’s trade name is Super Power Rail) variant for HPC. Our model shows Apple reclaims 67% node share on A14, its highest since N3.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: TSMC’s A16 is architected for HPC. Backside power delivery, gate-all-around transistors, and thermal envelopes are optimized for HPC and smartphones will skip this node. Nvidia benefits disproportionately. Apple’s N2 (N2 + A16) share drops not because Apple lost leverage, but because the node was built for a different customer. A14 (1.4nm) rebalances the equation. TSMC is designing A14 for both mobile and HPC from the start, with a separate backside power (TSMC’s trade name is Super Power Rail) variant for HPC. Our model shows Apple reclaims 67% node share on A14, its highest since N3.

Backside power delivery, gate-all-around transistors, and thermal envelopes are optimized for HPC and smartphones will skip this node. Nvidia benefits disproportionately. Apple’s N2 (N2 + A16) share drops not because Apple lost leverage, but because the node was built for a different customer. A14 (1.4nm) rebalances the equation. TSMC is designing A14 for both mobile and HPC from the start, with a separate backside power (TSMC’s trade name is Super Power Rail) variant for HPC. Our model shows Apple reclaims 67% node share on A14, its highest since N3.

pro.text: 台积电的 A16 节点（译注：此处指台积电的 1.6 纳米埃米级节点，而非苹果处理器）专为 HPC 架构而生。其背面供电、环绕栅极晶体管以及散热设计均针对 HPC 进行了深度优化，智能手机产品线将直接跳过该制程节点。英伟达从中获益尤为显著。苹果的 N2（N2 家族包含 N2 与 A16）份额之所以下滑，并非因为苹果失去了话语权，而是因为该制程节点本就为其他客户量身定制。A14（1.4 纳米）节点则将重新平衡这一格局。台积电从研发之初便让 A14 兼顾移动端与 HPC 需求，并专门为 HPC 打造了独立的背面供电（台积电的商业命名为超级电轨）版本。我们的模型显示，苹果将在 A14 节点上重新夺回 67% 的产能份额，创下自 N3 以来的新高。

paragraphs.source: Apple is not standing still. New chip families (N-series, C-series) account for 15% of wafer demand by 2030. iPhone’s share of Apple’s wafer mix fell from 74% to 57% as Mac silicon and custom chips scaled. Gross margins reflect in-house silicon transition. Mac GM expanded from 28.5% to 39.5% after ditching Intel, an 11-percentage point expansion. iPhone GM grew 5 percentage points from A4 to A18. Annual chip savings exceed $7B across Intel, Qualcomm, and Broadcom displacement. Apple has driven $300B+ in supplier capex over the past decade, building a supply chain empire that spans Foxconn, ASML, and dozens of precision manufacturers.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple is not standing still. New chip families (N-series, C-series) account for 15% of wafer demand by 2030. iPhone’s share of Apple’s wafer mix fell from 74% to 57% as Mac silicon and custom chips scaled. Gross margins reflect in-house silicon transition. Mac GM expanded from 28.5% to 39.5% after ditching Intel, an 11-percentage point expansion. iPhone GM grew 5 percentage points from A4 to A18. Annual chip savings exceed $7B across Intel, Qualcomm, and Broadcom displacement. Apple has driven $300B+ in supplier capex over the past decade, building a supply chain empire that spans Foxconn, ASML, and dozens of precision manufacturers.

Gross margins reflect in-house silicon transition. Mac GM expanded from 28.5% to 39.5% after ditching Intel, an 11-percentage point expansion. iPhone GM grew 5 percentage points from A4 to A18. Annual chip savings exceed $7B across Intel, Qualcomm, and Broadcom displacement. Apple has driven $300B+ in supplier capex over the past decade, building a supply chain empire that spans Foxconn, ASML, and dozens of precision manufacturers.

pro.text: 苹果也并未原地踏步。到 2030 年，全新芯片产品线（N系列、C系列）将占据其 15% 的晶圆需求。随着 Mac 自研芯片与定制芯片规模放量，iPhone 在苹果晶圆产品组合中的占比已从 74% 降至 57%。毛利率的提升直观印证了芯片自研战略的成效。在彻底弃用英特尔之后，Mac 的毛利率从 28.5% 跃升至 39.5%，实现了 11 个百分点的扩张。从 A4 到 A18 芯片，iPhone 的毛利率也增长了 5 个百分点。通过逐步替代英特尔、高通（Qualcomm）与博通的芯片，苹果每年的芯片成本节约超 70 亿美元。过去十年间，苹果累计拉动了超 3000 亿美元的供应商资本支出，构筑起一个横跨富士康、ASML 以及数十家精密制造企业的供应链帝国。

paragraphs.source: The analysis is built on two proprietary models. Our [Foundry Model](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/) tracks revenue, wafer shipments, and capacity allocation, quantifying the platform shift where smartphone revenue fell from 46% to 29% while HPC rose from 36% to 58%, and how Apple’s purchase obligations grew from $8.7B to $71B. Our [Apple Wafer Demand Model](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/) forecasts silicon demand across chip families (A-series, M-series, S-series, N-series, C-series etc.), calculates gross margin savings exceeding $8B annually, and projects Apple’s leading-edge share declining from near 100% at N3 to <50% at N2 as AI accelerators reshape allocation.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The analysis is built on two proprietary models. Our **[Foundry Model](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/)** tracks revenue, wafer shipments, and capacity allocation, quantifying the platform shift where smartphone revenue fell from 46% to 29% while HPC rose from 36% to 58%, and how Apple’s purchase obligations grew from $8.7B to $71B. Our **[Apple Wafer Demand Model](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/)** forecasts silicon demand across chip families (A-series, M-series, S-series, N-series, C-series etc.), calculates gross margin savings exceeding $8B annually, and projects Apple’s leading-edge share declining from near 100% at N3 to <50% at N2 as AI accelerators reshape allocation. The report concludes with Taiwan risk scenarios, Arizona economics, and conditions under which Apple would build its own fabs.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The analysis is built on two proprietary models. Our [Foundry Model](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/) tracks revenue, wafer shipments, and capacity allocation, quantifying the platform shift where smartphone revenue fell from 46% to 29% while HPC rose from 36% to 58%, and how Apple’s purchase obligations grew from $8.7B to $71B. Our [Apple Wafer Demand Model](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/) forecasts silicon demand across chip families (A-series, M-series, S-series, N-series, C-series etc.), calculates gross margin savings exceeding $8B annually, and projects Apple’s leading-edge share declining from near 100% at N3 to <50% at N2 as AI accelerators reshape allocation. The report concludes with Taiwan risk scenarios, Arizona economics, and conditions under which Apple would build its own fabs.

pro.text: 本篇分析基于两大独家模型。我们的[晶圆代工模型](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/)追踪了营收、晶圆出货量与产能分配情况。该模型量化了终端平台的更迭进程——智能手机营收占比从 46% 跌至 29%，而 HPC 占比则从 36% 跃升至 58%；同时还原了苹果采购承诺从 87 亿美元攀升至 710 亿美元的完整轨迹。我们的[苹果晶圆需求模型](https://semianalysis.com/foundry-industry-model/)预测了各大芯片产品线（A系列、M系列、S系列、N系列、C系列等）的硅片需求。该模型计算出苹果每年由此带来的毛利提升超过 80 亿美元，并预测在 AI 加速器重塑产能分配的背景下，苹果的先进制程份额将从 N3 节点的近乎 100% 跌至 N2 节点的 50% 以下。

applied_terms: Translate 'yield learning' as 良率爬坡 or 良率学习曲线.
Translate 'product cadence' as 产品发布节奏 or 产品迭代步调.
Ensure the translation captures the severity of 'switching costs' (转换成本) implied by the 2-3 year penalty.

understanding.role_in_article: Chronologically maps the evolution of the alliance to highlight the immense switching costs and lock-in effects that prevent Apple from easily moving to competitors.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Explains the historical timeline of *how* the massive wafer demand and financial metrics from the previous sections were achieved.
understanding.relation_to_next: The concept of historical lock-in naturally introduces the physical and technical lock-in discussed in 'The Manufacturing Footprint'.
key_points: Apple's product cadence is deeply synchronized with TSMC's node roadmap.
Switching to Intel or Samsung carries a severe 2-3 year penalty of inferior products due to yield learning curves.
The annual iPhone holiday refresh cycle relies entirely on this synchronized execution.

issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。
中英文排版规范细节：译文“M系列和 A系列”中，“M”与“系列”之间没有空格，而“A”与前面的“和”之间有空格，排版不够统一。

paragraphs.source: The original iPhone launched in 2007 relied heavily on Samsung components. Samsung supplied the application processor, display, and flash memory. While making its chips an inevitable destiny for Apple, a few things accelerated the journey.

paragraphs.source: First, Apple’s key supplier Samsung entered smartphones 18 months after iPhone launched, ending up in a legal battle with Apple as early Galaxy S designs resembled iPhones. The unease grew with each Samsung’s release and its growing competitive position smartphones, pushing Apple to look for alternatives.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: **First**, Apple’s key supplier Samsung entered smartphones 18 months after iPhone launched, ending up in a legal battle with Apple as early Galaxy S designs resembled iPhones. The unease grew with each Samsung’s release and its growing competitive position smartphones, pushing Apple to look for alternatives.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: First, Apple’s key supplier Samsung entered smartphones 18 months after iPhone launched, ending up in a legal battle with Apple as early Galaxy S designs resembled iPhones. The unease grew with each Samsung’s release and its growing competitive position smartphones, pushing Apple to look for alternatives.

pro.text: 第一，苹果的核心供应商三星在 iPhone 发布 18 个月后进军智能手机市场，由于早期的 Galaxy S 设计酷似 iPhone，双方最终陷入法律纠纷。随着三星每次发布新机及其在智能手机领域的竞争地位不断攀升，苹果的不安感与日俱增，迫使其寻找替代方案。

paragraphs.source: Second, the rise of the Wintel model (Android plus Qualcomm) in smartphones in the 2010s worried Apple that commodity chips could erode its differentiation in software and affect its premium status. Jobs made the call in 2008: Apple would design its own chips. But manufacturing requires 10s of billions in fabs. So Apple adopted a fabless systems company approach.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: **Second**, the rise of the Wintel model (Android plus Qualcomm) in smartphones in the 2010s worried Apple that commodity chips could erode its differentiation in software and affect its premium status. Jobs made the call in 2008: Apple would design its own chips. But manufacturing requires 10s of billions in fabs. So Apple adopted a fabless systems company approach.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Second, the rise of the Wintel model (Android plus Qualcomm) in smartphones in the 2010s worried Apple that commodity chips could erode its differentiation in software and affect its premium status. Jobs made the call in 2008: Apple would design its own chips. But manufacturing requires 10s of billions in fabs. So Apple adopted a fabless systems company approach.

pro.text: 第二，2010 年代智能手机领域“Wintel”模式（安卓加高通）的崛起让苹果感到担忧，害怕通用芯片会削弱其在软件方面的差异化优势，并影响其高端定位。乔布斯在 2008 年拍板决定：苹果将自研芯片。但制造芯片需要耗资数百亿美元建设晶圆厂。因此，苹果采取了无晶圆厂（fabless）系统公司模式。

paragraphs.source: Apple wanted to control the primary technologies used in its devices. The $278M P.A. Semi acquisition in April 2008 served as a stepping stone. Dan Dobberpuhl, creator of Alpha and StrongARM, had assembled 150 of the best low-power chip engineers in the world. Among them was Johny Srouji, an Israeli engineer from Intel and IBM. He now runs Apple Silicon as SVP of Hardware Technologies.

He now runs Apple Silicon as SVP of Hardware Technologies.

pro.text: 苹果渴望掌控其设备搭载的核心技术。2008 年 4 月斥资 2.78 亿美元收购 P.A. Semi 便是关键的垫脚石。Alpha 和 StrongARM 的缔造者 Dan Dobberpuhl 汇聚了全球 150 位最顶尖的低功耗芯片工程师。曾在英特尔和 IBM 任职的以色列工程师 Johny Srouji 便是其中之一。他如今作为硬件技术高级副总裁掌管 Apple Silicon。

paragraphs.source: Following P.A. Semi, Apple acquired ultra-low-power chip design company Intrinsity for $121M in 2010 Apple fielded its first custom smartphone applications processor (AP), the A4, in iPhone 4 in September 2010. While it was still manufactured by Samsung, Apple intensified its search for a manufacturing partner who wasn’t also their competitor.

pro.text: 继 P.A. Semi 之后，苹果于 2010 年斥资 1.21 亿美元收购了超低功耗芯片设计公司 Intrinsity。2010 年 9 月，苹果在 iPhone 4 中搭载了其首款定制智能手机应用处理器（AP）A4。尽管该芯片仍由三星代工，但苹果开始加紧寻找一家非竞争对手的制造合作伙伴。

paragraphs.source: Enter Intel and TSMC, two of the leading fab options for Apple. Discussions with Intel were unsuccessful as then CEO Paul Otellini declined, believing the volume wouldn’t justify the low margin and rigorous demands Apple placed on suppliers. At TSMC, Morris Chang accepted the challenge, viewing it as a growth opportunity rather than a margin drag.

At TSMC, Morris Chang accepted the challenge, viewing it as a growth opportunity rather than a margin drag.

Discussions with Intel were unsuccessful as then CEO Paul Otellini declined, believing the volume wouldn’t justify the low margin and rigorous demands Apple placed on suppliers. At TSMC, Morris Chang accepted the challenge, viewing it as a growth opportunity rather than a margin drag.

pro.text: 英特尔和台积电这两大顶尖晶圆厂选项随之入局。苹果与英特尔的谈判无果而终，时任首席执行官 Paul Otellini 拒绝了合作，他笃定苹果的订单量不足以弥补其低利润率，也不值得为之承受苹果对供应商的严苛要求。而在台积电，张忠谋接下了这一挑战，将其视为业务增长的机遇，而非拖累利润率的包袱。

issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。
中英文排版规范细节：译文“M系列和 A系列”中，“M”与“系列”之间没有空格，而“A”与前面的“和”之间有空格，排版不够统一。

paragraphs.source: Apple COO Jeff Williams met with Chang over dinner and pitched TSMC to build 20nm capacity. At that time, TSMC was shifting focus and investment to 16nm. The capital and capacity numbers Apple asked were unheard of; they went so far as suggesting TSMC cut its dividend to fund the fab buildout. TSMC made the bet. They were able to fund the fabs with debt. At the time of the initial decision, success was far from a sure thing for either side.

pro.text: 苹果首席运营官 Jeff Williams 在晚宴上与张忠谋会面，游说台积电建设 20 纳米产能。彼时，台积电正将重心与投资转向 16 纳米。苹果要求的资本开支与产能规模前所未闻；他们甚至提议台积电削减股息，以此为晶圆厂建设筹资。台积电押下了这一注。他们最终通过举债为晶圆厂筹集了资金。在最初拍板之际，对双方而言，成功都绝非板上钉钉。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s A8 chip launched in 2014, and TSMC never looked back. Over the next six years, Apple drove TSMC to invest $60-80 billion in leading-edge capacity. Apple’s volume justified every major node transition: N16, N7, N5. Without the iPhone’s annual 200M unit baseline, TSMC could not have afforded the R&D velocity that left Intel and Samsung behind.

pro.text: 苹果 A8 芯片于 2014 年发布，台积电从此一路高歌猛进。在接下来的六年里，苹果推动台积电在先进制程产能上投资了 600 亿至 800 亿美元。苹果的庞大体量支撑了每一次重大的制程节点迭代：N16、N7、N5。如果没有 iPhone 每年两亿部出货量的基本盘，台积电根本无力承担如此惊人的研发迭代速度，更无法将英特尔和三星甩在身后。

paragraphs.source: In 2016, Apple funded the development of InFO (Integrated Fan-Out) packaging. This allowed for thinner phones with better thermals and created the advanced packaging ecosystem that now powers AI accelerators.

pro.text: 2016 年，苹果出资推动了 InFO 封装的研发。这不仅打造出机身更轻薄、散热更出色的手机，更催生了先进封装生态系统，如今正是该生态系统在为 AI 加速器提供强大动力。

paragraphs.source: At the core there are cultural differences: Intel, trapped in its product success, couldn’t foresee the massive Arm-based foundry market; TSMC offered servitude, flexibility, and a willingness to ‘bet the company’ on Apple’s success. TSMC built dedicated capacity, accepted Apple’s engineers into their labs, and aligned their roadmap with the iPhone’s annual cycle. This cultural alignment, TSMC’s willingness to customize versus Intel’s standardized product approach, remains Intel’s biggest hurdle in its IDM 2.0 pivot today.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: At the core there are cultural differences: Intel, trapped in its product success, couldn’t foresee the massive Arm-based foundry market; TSMC offered servitude, flexibility, and a willingness to ‘bet the company’ on Apple’s success. TSMC built dedicated capacity, accepted Apple’s engineers into their labs, and aligned their roadmap with the iPhone’s annual cycle. This cultural alignment, TSMC’s willingness to customize versus Intel’s standardized product approach, remains Intel’s biggest hurdle in its IDM 2.0 pivot today.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: At the core there are cultural differences: Intel, trapped in its product success, couldn’t foresee the massive Arm-based foundry market; TSMC offered servitude, flexibility, and a willingness to ‘bet the company’ on Apple’s success. TSMC built dedicated capacity, accepted Apple’s engineers into their labs, and aligned their roadmap with the iPhone’s annual cycle. This cultural alignment, TSMC’s willingness to customize versus Intel’s standardized product approach, remains Intel’s biggest hurdle in its IDM 2.0 pivot today.

pro.text: 核心在于文化差异：英特尔受困于自身产品的成功光环，未能预见基于 Arm 架构的庞大晶圆代工市场；台积电则展现出极致的服务姿态与灵活性，甚至拿“全公司的命运”押注苹果的成功。台积电为苹果建设了专属产能，允许苹果工程师进驻其实验室，并将自身的技术路线图与 iPhone 的年度迭代周期深度绑定。这种文化上的契合度，体现为台积电甘愿定制的姿态与英特尔死守标准化产品的模式截然不同。时至今日，这依然是英特尔向 IDM 2.0 转型过程中的最大障碍。

paragraphs.source: Despite investing in dedicated 20nm capacity for Apple, TSMC did not even receive a majority share of Apple’s foundry business at first. It had to share a 14nm slot with Samsung in 2015 with the latter getting >60% share. TSMC management was shocked, but responded by accelerating their next-gen 10nm process.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Despite investing in dedicated 20nm capacity for Apple, TSMC did not even receive a majority share of Apple’s foundry business at first. It had to share a 14nm slot with Samsung in 2015 with the latter getting >60% share. TSMC management was shocked, but responded by accelerating their next-gen 10nm process.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Despite investing in dedicated 20nm capacity for Apple, TSMC did not even receive a majority share of Apple’s foundry business at first. It had to share a 14nm slot with Samsung in 2015 with the latter getting >60% share. TSMC management was shocked, but responded by accelerating their next-gen 10nm process.

pro.text: 尽管台积电为苹果投资建设了 20 纳米专属产能，起初却连苹果晶圆代工业务的大头都没能拿下。2015 年，台积电不得不与三星瓜分 14 纳米订单，且后者抢走了超过 60% 的份额。台积电管理层大为震惊，但随即予以反击：加速推进下一代 10 纳米工艺。

paragraphs.source: Ultimately, TSMC won because it demonstrated the ability to scale 20nm ahead of the competition, proving it could handle the iPhone’s massive volume spikes. The “Night Hawk” team at TSMC worked 24/7 to solve yield issues, establishing the operational trust that persists today.

The “Night Hawk” team at TSMC worked 24/7 to solve yield issues, establishing the operational trust that persists today.

pro.text: 台积电最终胜出，是因为它展现出领先于竞争对手扩产 20 纳米工艺的能力，证明自身能够应对 iPhone 庞大的订单激增。台积电的“夜鹰”团队全天候连轴转，攻克良率难题，建立起了延续至今的运营信任。

paragraphs.source: What if Apple chose Intel in 2014? Intel would have $15B+/year guaranteed foundry revenue. TSMC, without that revenue, would probably not have achieved dominance anywhere near what it has today. Intel Foundry would be 10 years more mature. It’s the biggest misstep in the history of chip foundries.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: What if Apple chose Intel in 2014? Intel would have $15B+/year guaranteed foundry revenue. TSMC, without that revenue, would probably not have achieved dominance anywhere near what it has today. Intel Foundry would be 10 years more mature. It’s the biggest misstep in the history of chip foundries.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: What if Apple chose Intel in 2014? Intel would have $15B+/year guaranteed foundry revenue. It’s the biggest misstep in the history of chip foundries.

pro.text: 试想，如果苹果在 2014 年选择了英特尔会怎样？英特尔每年将获得超过 150 亿美元的保底晶圆代工营收。失去这笔营收的台积电，恐怕根本无法企及如今的霸主地位。英特尔的晶圆代工业务则会比现在成熟十年。这是芯片代工史上最大的战略失误。

Switching to Intel or Samsung carries a severe 2-3 year penalty of inferior products due to yield learning curves.

issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。
中英文排版规范细节：译文“M系列和 A系列”中，“M”与“系列”之间没有空格，而“A”与前面的“和”之间有空格，排版不够统一。

paragraphs.source: By 2020, the partnership had evolved from mutually beneficial to co-dependence. Apple could no longer leave. No other foundry on earth could produce M-series and A-series chips at the required volume and yield. Samsung’s 3nm yields were 30-40% versus TSMC’s 80%+. The switching cost was estimated at $2-5 billion in redesign and requalification alone.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: By 2020, the partnership had evolved from mutually beneficial to co-dependence. Apple could no longer leave. No other foundry on earth could produce M-series and A-series chips at the required volume and yield. Samsung’s 3nm yields were 30-40% versus TSMC’s 80%+. The switching cost was estimated at $2-5 billion in redesign and requalification alone.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: By 2020, the partnership had evolved from mutually beneficial to co-dependence. Apple could no longer leave. No other foundry on earth could produce M-series and A-series chips at the required volume and yield. Samsung’s 3nm yields were 30-40% versus TSMC’s 80%+. The switching cost was estimated at $2-5 billion in redesign and requalification alone.

pro.text: 到 2020 年，双方的合作关系已从互利共赢演变为相互依存。苹果再也无法抽身。放眼全球，没有任何一家其他晶圆代工厂能在产能和良率上满足 M系列和 A系列芯片的要求。三星的 3 纳米良率仅为 30% 至 40%，而台积电则高达 80% 以上。仅重新设计和重新认证带来的转换成本，估计就高达 20 亿至 50 亿美元。

paragraphs.source: TSMC could not lose Apple. The iPhone brought 22-25% of total revenue and filled 70%+ of 3nm capacity. Apple orders were known three years in advance, allowing TSMC to plan capex with the confidence of a utility company.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: TSMC could not lose Apple. The iPhone brought 22-25% of total revenue and filled 70%+ of 3nm capacity. Apple orders were known three years in advance, allowing TSMC to plan capex with the confidence of a utility company.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: TSMC could not lose Apple. The iPhone brought 22-25% of total revenue and filled 70%+ of 3nm capacity. Apple orders were known three years in advance, allowing TSMC to plan capex with the confidence of a utility company.

pro.text: 台积电同样不能失去苹果。iPhone 贡献了其总营收的 22% 至 25%，并填补了超过 70% 的 3 纳米产能。苹果的订单会提前三年敲定，这让台积电能像公用事业公司一样，底气十足地规划资本支出。

paragraphs.source: A more serious risk is timing. Apple’s product cadence is synchronized to TSMC’s node roadmap. Moving to Intel or Samsung would mean 2-3 years of inferior products while yield learning catches up. Product upgrade cycles, most importantly the annual iPhone refresh synced with holidays, would also be at risk.

pro.text: 更严峻的风险在于时间节点。苹果的产品迭代步调与台积电的制程节点路线图高度同步。若转向英特尔或三星，意味着在良率爬坡追赶期间，苹果将面临长达两三年的产品劣势。产品升级周期也将受到威胁，尤其是与假日季同步的 iPhone 年度更新。

issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。

paragraphs.source: For years, Hsinchu and Cupertino worked as a single team to relentlessly advance Moore’s Law. Apple’s One Team approach embedded hundreds of engineers at TSMC’s headquarters, effectively treating the foundry as an extension of Cupertino. This team co-developed Process Design Kits (PDKs), ensuring that when a new node like 5nm launched, Apple’s designs were perfectly synthesized to the transistor characteristics.

Apple’s One Team approach embedded hundreds of engineers at TSMC’s headquarters, effectively treating the foundry as an extension of Cupertino. This team co-developed Process Design Kits (PDKs), ensuring that when a new node like 5nm launched, Apple’s designs were perfectly synthesized to the transistor characteristics.

pro.text: 多年来，新竹与库比蒂诺宛如一体，不遗余力地推动摩尔定律向前发展。苹果采用“同一团队”（One Team）模式，将数百名工程师派驻台积电总部，实际上已将这家晶圆代工厂视为库比蒂诺的延伸。该团队共同开发工艺设计套件（PDK），确保当 5 纳米等新制程节点发布时，苹果的设计能与晶体管特性完美契合。

paragraphs.source: However, the rise of generative AI is shifting TSMC’s customer mix. While Apple remains the largest single customer by revenue, its relative influence is diluting as the High-Performance Computing (HPC) segment, driven by NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscalers, outgrows. In Q1 2020, smartphones accounted for 49% of TSMC’s revenue, while HPC was 30%. By Q3 2025, HPC had skyrocketed to 57%, relegating smartphones to a secondary growth driver.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: However, the rise of generative AI is shifting TSMC’s customer mix. While Apple remains the largest single customer by revenue, its relative influence is diluting as the High-Performance Computing (HPC) segment, driven by NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscalers, outgrows. In Q1 2020, smartphones accounted for 49% of TSMC’s revenue, while HPC was 30%. By Q3 2025, HPC had skyrocketed to 57%, relegating smartphones to a secondary growth driver.

In Q1 2020, smartphones accounted for 49% of TSMC’s revenue, while HPC was 30%. By Q3 2025, HPC had skyrocketed to 57%, relegating smartphones to a secondary growth driver.

pro.text: 然而，生成式 AI 的崛起正在重塑台积电的客户结构。尽管按营收计算，苹果依然是最大的单一客户，但在英伟达、AMD 以及超大规模云厂商的驱动下，HPC 业务板块的增长势头更为迅猛，苹果的相对影响力正被稀释。2020 年第一季度，智能手机占台积电营收的 49%，而 HPC 占 30%。到了 2025 年第三季度，HPC 占比已飙升至 57%，智能手机退居为次要增长引擎。

paragraphs.source: While Apple also contributes to TSMC’s HPC segment through its tablet/PC chips, the uptake of AI offered TSMC a new stream of customers who are voracious for advanced node capacity. While AI accelerators moved 1 year cadence, they are still on n-1 node largely. Apple will still be the anchor customer on N2 (2nm) but it will see stiff competition from others competing for capacity. On A16 (1.6nm), HPC players are likely to beat Apple as it is a more HPC centric node.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: While Apple also contributes to TSMC’s HPC segment through its tablet/PC chips, the uptake of AI offered TSMC a new stream of customers who are voracious for advanced node capacity. While AI accelerators moved 1 year cadence, they are still on n-1 node largely. Apple will still be the anchor customer on N2 (2nm) but it will see stiff competition from others competing for capacity. On A16 (1.6nm), HPC players are likely to beat Apple as it is a more HPC centric node.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: While Apple also contributes to TSMC’s HPC segment through its tablet/PC chips, the uptake of AI offered TSMC a new stream of customers who are voracious for advanced node capacity. While AI accelerators moved 1 year cadence, they are still on n-1 node largely. Apple will still be the anchor customer on N2 (2nm) but it will see stiff competition from others competing for capacity. On A16 (1.6nm), HPC players are likely to beat Apple as it is a more HPC centric node.

pro.text: 尽管苹果的平板电脑与 PC 芯片同样为台积电的 HPC 业务做出了贡献，但 AI 的爆发为台积电带来了一批对先进制程节点产能如饥似渴的新客户。虽然 AI 加速器已转向一年一更的迭代步调，但它们目前主要仍在使用 n-1 节点。苹果依然会是 N2（2 纳米）的基石客户，但将面临其他争夺产能者的激烈竞争。而在 A16（台积电 1.6 纳米埃米级节点，而非苹果处理器）上，由于该节点更侧重于 HPC，HPC 厂商很可能会击败苹果抢占先机。

pro.text: 苹果充当着可预测的业绩底盘，为新建晶圆厂庞大的固定成本提供了合理性支撑。英伟达则带来了高毛利的上升空间，驱动利润率增长。如今，台积电已拥有两大基石客户，而非一家独大。

issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。
中英文排版规范细节：译文“M系列和 A系列”中，“M”与“系列”之间没有空格，而“A”与前面的“和”之间有空格，排版不够统一。

paragraphs.source: The distinction lies in what these customers are buying. Apple buys leading-edge logic wafers (N3, N3E) and InFO (Integrated Fan-Out) packaging. NVIDIA buys logic wafers (N4, N5) on a custom process a node or 2 behind leading edge but is critically dependent on CoWoS (Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate) packaging.

NVIDIA buys logic wafers (N4, N5) on a custom process a node or 2 behind leading edge but is critically dependent on CoWoS (Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate) packaging.

pro.text: 区别在于这些客户购买的产品。苹果购买先进制程逻辑晶圆（N3、N3E）和 InFO 封装。英伟达购买的逻辑晶圆（N4、N5）采用定制工艺，落后先进制程一两个制程节点，但对 CoWoS封装的依赖极高。

paragraphs.source: Apple was TSMC’s first advanced packaging customer at scale. InFO revenue grew from $1.8B in 2018 to >$3.5B in 2024, driven entirely by A-series and M-series chips. But CoWoS, TSMC’s AI packaging platform, has surpassed it. CoWoS revenue hit $9.6B in 2025, 2.5x InFO, fueled by Nvidia and AMD demand.

CoWoS revenue hit $9.6B in 2025, 2.5x InFO, fueled by Nvidia and AMD demand.

pro.text: 苹果是台积电首个实现规模化采用先进封装的客户。在 A系列和 M系列芯片的全面驱动下，InFO 营收从 2018 年的 18 亿美元增长至 2024 年的超 35 亿美元。然而，台积电的 AI 封装平台 CoWoS 现已实现反超。在英伟达和 AMD 需求的推动下，2025 年 CoWoS 营收达到 96 亿美元，是 InFO 的 2.5 倍。

paragraphs.source: This creates a bifurcation in TSMC’s capacity planning. Apple is no longer the sole driver of capex. TSMC’s capital expenditure is now split between chasing Moore’s Law (2nm for Apple) and chasing packaging density (CoWoS-L for NVIDIA). Apple acts as the predictable baseline that justifies the massive fixed costs of new fabs, while NVIDIA provides the high-margin upside that drives profitability growth. The power dynamic has shifted from a unipolar world (Apple) to a bipolar world (Apple + AI), where TSMC can now arbitrage demand between the two sectors to maintain pricing power.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: This creates a bifurcation in TSMC’s capacity planning. Apple is no longer the sole driver of capex. TSMC’s capital expenditure is now split between chasing Moore’s Law (2nm for Apple) and chasing packaging density (CoWoS-L for NVIDIA). Apple acts as the predictable baseline that justifies the massive fixed costs of new fabs, while NVIDIA provides the high-margin upside that drives profitability growth. The power dynamic has shifted from a unipolar world (Apple) to a bipolar world (Apple + AI), where TSMC can now arbitrage demand between the two sectors to maintain pricing power.

TSMC’s capital expenditure is now split between chasing Moore’s Law (2nm for Apple) and chasing packaging density (CoWoS-L for NVIDIA). Apple acts as the predictable baseline that justifies the massive fixed costs of new fabs, while NVIDIA provides the high-margin upside that drives profitability growth. The power dynamic has shifted from a unipolar world (Apple) to a bipolar world (Apple + AI), where TSMC can now arbitrage demand between the two sectors to maintain pricing power.

pro.text: 这导致台积电的产能规划出现分化。苹果不再是资本支出的唯一驱动力。台积电目前的资本支出被一分为二：一部分用于推进摩尔定律（为苹果研发 2nm），另一部分用于提升封装密度（为英伟达扩充 CoWoS-L）。苹果充当着可预测的业绩底盘，为新建晶圆厂庞大的固定成本提供了合理性支撑。英伟达则带来了高毛利的上升空间，驱动利润率增长。权力格局已从单极世界（苹果）转向双极世界（苹果加 AI），台积电如今可以在两大领域的不同需求之间进行套利，借此维持定价权。

issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。
中英文排版规范细节：译文“M系列和 A系列”中，“M”与“系列”之间没有空格，而“A”与前面的“和”之间有空格，排版不够统一。

paragraphs.source: Intel’s 18A-P process (shipping late 2026) represents the first theoretically viable alternative since Apple left Samsung in 2016. Apple could qualify Intel, initially for lower-risk silicon such as base M-series, and yield well. That would give Intel reference design wins and Apple supply chain diversification without risking core products.

pro.text: 英特尔18A-P工艺（将于2026年底出货）是自2016年苹果离开三星以来，首个在理论上可行的替代方案。苹果可以对英特尔进行资质认证，初期先将其用于基础款M系列等低风险芯片，并取得不错的良率。如此一来，英特尔将斩获标杆客户订单，苹果也能在不危及核心产品的前提下实现供应链多元化。

paragraphs.source: But, 18A-P on base M-series chips makes sense. If Apple were to shift 20% of base M-series wafers to Intel 18A-P, it would imply $630M in foundry revenue for Intel at an $18k ASP.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: But, 18A-P on base M-series chips makes sense. If Apple were to shift 20% of base M-series wafers to Intel 18A-P, it would imply $630M in foundry revenue for Intel at an $18k ASP.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: But, 18A-P on base M-series chips makes sense. If Apple were to shift 20% of base M-series wafers to Intel 18A-P, it would imply $630M in foundry revenue for Intel at an $18k ASP.

pro.text: 不过，将18A-P用于基础款M系列芯片确实合乎逻辑。如果苹果将20%的基础款M系列晶圆订单转移给英特尔18A-P，按1.8万美元的平均售价 (ASP) 计算，这将为英特尔带来6.3亿美元的晶圆代工厂营收。

paragraphs.source: While Intel’s current 18A yields are lower than TSMC’s N3 for a 150mm² die (>80%), Intel offers pricing leverage, potential 14A optionality, and US-based wafer/packaging capabilities for Apple.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: While Intel’s current 18A yields are lower than TSMC’s N3 for a 150mm² die (>80%), Intel offers pricing leverage, potential 14A optionality, and US-based wafer/packaging capabilities for Apple.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: While Intel’s current 18A yields are lower than TSMC’s N3 for a 150mm² die (>80%), Intel offers pricing leverage, potential 14A optionality, and US-based wafer/packaging capabilities for Apple.

pro.text: 尽管对于150平方毫米的裸片，目前英特尔18A的良率仍低于台积电N3（大于80%），但英特尔能为苹果提供议价筹码、潜在的14A工艺选择权，以及美国本土的晶圆制造与封装能力。

pro.text: 相比采用背面供电（PowerVia）的英特尔18A，18A-P的每瓦性能提升了8%，且晶体管密度相近。

issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。
中英文排版规范细节：译文“M系列和 A系列”中，“M”与“系列”之间没有空格，而“A”与前面的“和”之间有空格，排版不够统一。

paragraphs.source: There are a few lower impact possibilities too: while the Intel16 mature node in its Ireland fab could address DTV and connectivity apps, we doubt the capacity is enough to serve Apple. Apple could qualify Intel for lower-risk silicon: WiFi/Bluetooth, display drivers, or power management. That would give Intel reference design wins and Apple supply chain diversification without risking core products.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: There are a few lower impact possibilities too: while the Intel16 mature node in its Ireland fab could address DTV and connectivity apps, we doubt the capacity is enough to serve Apple. Apple could qualify Intel for lower-risk silicon: WiFi/Bluetooth, display drivers, or power management. That would give Intel reference design wins and Apple supply chain diversification without risking core products.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: There are a few lower impact possibilities too: while the Intel16 mature node in its Ireland fab could address DTV and connectivity apps, we doubt the capacity is enough to serve Apple. Apple could qualify Intel for lower-risk silicon: WiFi/Bluetooth, display drivers, or power management. That would give Intel reference design wins and Apple supply chain diversification without risking core products.

pro.text: 此外还有一些影响较小的备选方案：虽然英特尔爱尔兰晶圆厂的Intel16成熟制程节点足以应对数字电视 (DTV) 和连接应用，但我们怀疑其产能无法满足苹果的需求。苹果可对英特尔的低风险芯片进行认证，例如WiFi/蓝牙芯片、显示驱动芯片或电源管理芯片。这既能让英特尔获得标杆客户背书，又能让苹果在不危及核心产品的前提下实现供应链多元化。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s actual foundry diversification isn’t about moving leading-edge A-series/M-series away from TSMC. Non-Pro versions, Peripheral chips and packaging are all candidates for foundry diversification.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple’s actual foundry diversification isn’t about moving leading-edge A-series/M-series away from TSMC. Non-Pro versions, Peripheral chips and packaging are all candidates for foundry diversification.

pro.text: 苹果实际的晶圆代工厂多元化战略，并非将先进制程的A系列和M系列订单撤出台积电。非Pro版芯片、外围芯片以及封装业务，才是多元化战略的真正候选目标。

issues.description: 原文的 'share a 14nm slot' 是一种宽泛的说法（指代同一代际的代工位置），但台积电当时为苹果代工 A9 芯片实际使用的是 16nm 工艺，三星使用的是 14nm 工艺。译文直译为“瓜分 14 纳米订单”会给半导体专业读者造成“台积电当时也生产了 14nm 芯片”的技术误解。
“filled... capacity” 在制造业和代工语境下指“填满/占据/消化产能”，译文使用“填补”不够准确。“填补”通常指弥补空缺或亏空（如填补空白、填补亏损），在此处搭配略显生硬。
中英文排版规范细节：译文“M系列和 A系列”中，“M”与“系列”之间没有空格，而“A”与前面的“和”之间有空格，排版不够统一。

paragraphs.source: Apple has signed a strategic deal with Samsung Foundry to manufacture advanced CMOS Image Sensors (CIS) at Samsung’s Austin, Texas facilities, breaking Sony’s decade-long exclusivity on iPhone image sensors. Leveraging Samsung’s US fabs helps Apple meet internal “American Manufacturing” targets without relying solely on TSMC Arizona, which is focused on leading-edge.

pro.text: 苹果已与三星晶圆代工厂签署战略协议，由三星德克萨斯州奥斯汀工厂代工先进CMOS图像传感器 (CIS)。此举打破了索尼十年来在 iPhone 图像传感器领域的独家垄断地位。借助三星的美国晶圆厂，苹果能够顺利推进内部的“美国制造”目标，无需将重注全压在专攻先进制程的台积电亚利桑那州工厂上。

paragraphs.source: To understand the relationship, one must understand the physical reality of where these chips are made. TSMC’s dispersed network of GigaFabs, each specializing in specific nodes and packaging technologies, churn out over a billion chips for Apple each year. Apple’s supply chain is deeply entrenched in Taiwan’s western corridor, but geopolitical hedging is forcing a costly expansion to Arizona.

pro.text: 要理解双方的合作关系，必须先厘清芯片制造的物理分布。台积电的超大型晶圆厂（GigaFab）网络分布广泛，各厂专攻特定的制程节点与封装技术，每年为苹果量产逾十亿颗芯片。苹果的供应链深度扎根于台湾西部走廊，但出于地缘政治避险考量，目前正被迫向美国亚利桑那州进行成本高昂的产能扩张。

paragraphs.source: Advanced packaging is an area where the divergence between Apple and NVIDIA is most acute. Apple relies on InFO-PoP (Integrated Fan-Out Package-on-Package), which stacks DRAM directly on top of the SoC to minimize thickness for iPhones. This technology was pivotal in Apple’s decision to leave Samsung, as it allowed for thinner phones with better thermal dissipation. NVIDIA, on the other hand, relies on CoWoS, which places HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) side-by-side with the GPU on a silicon interposer for maximum bandwidth.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Advanced packaging is an area where the divergence between Apple and NVIDIA is most acute. Apple relies on **InFO-PoP** (Integrated Fan-Out Package-on-Package), which stacks DRAM directly on top of the SoC to minimize thickness for iPhones. This technology was pivotal in Apple’s decision to leave Samsung, as it allowed for thinner phones with better thermal dissipation. NVIDIA, on the other hand, relies on **CoWoS**, which places HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) side-by-side with the GPU on a silicon interposer for maximum bandwidth.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Advanced packaging is an area where the divergence between Apple and NVIDIA is most acute. Apple relies on InFO-PoP (Integrated Fan-Out Package-on-Package), which stacks DRAM directly on top of the SoC to minimize thickness for iPhones. This technology was pivotal in Apple’s decision to leave Samsung, as it allowed for thinner phones with better thermal dissipation. NVIDIA, on the other hand, relies on CoWoS, which places HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) side-by-side with the GPU on a silicon interposer for maximum bandwidth.

pro.text: 在先进封装领域，苹果与英伟达的需求分化最为剧烈。苹果依赖集成扇出型层叠封装 (InFO-PoP)。该技术将 DRAM 直接堆叠在系统级芯片 (SoC) 上方，以最大限度降低 iPhone 厚度。这项技术是苹果当年决定弃用三星的关键，它不仅让手机更轻薄，还提升了散热性能。相比之下，英伟达则依赖CoWoS技术。该技术将 HBM 与 GPU 并排平铺于硅中介层上，以榨取最大带宽。

paragraphs.source: Apple and NVIDIA do not compete for the same packaging lines today. Apple dominates AP3 (InFO), while NVIDIA fights for AP5/AP6 (CoWoS). However, as Apple moves toward M5/M6 Ultra chips utilizing SoIC (System on Integrated Chips) and WMCM, they will begin to compete for the same advanced 3D packaging resources in AP6 and AP7. This convergence of roadmaps poses a future risk for capacity allocation.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple and NVIDIA do not compete for the same packaging lines today. Apple dominates AP3 (InFO), while NVIDIA fights for AP5/AP6 (CoWoS). This convergence of roadmaps poses a future risk for capacity allocation.

pro.text: 目前，苹果与英伟达尚未在同一条封装产线上短兵相接。苹果独占 AP3 厂（先进封装厂）的 InFO 产能，而英伟达则在争夺 AP5 和 AP6 厂的 CoWoS 产能。然而，随着苹果转向采用系统级整合芯片（SoIC）和 WMCM 技术的 M5/M6 Ultra 芯片，双方必将在 AP6 和 AP7 厂争夺相同的先进 3D 封装资源。这种技术路线的趋同，为未来的产能分配埋下了隐患。

This concentration in southern Taiwan is one of the key geopolitical vulnerabilities in Apple’s supply chain.

sections.title: Apple’s Five Transformational Chip Acquisitions
sections.title_translation: 苹果的五次变革性芯片收购

paragraphs.source: Apple’s overarching semiconductor philosophy is simple but ruthless: control the “primary technologies” that differentiate the product. This journey, which began with the A4 in 2010, has evolved into a comprehensive internalization strategy. It’s not just about the CPU (A-series/M-series); Apple has systematically replaced suppliers for almost every critical subsystem, developing custom silicon for Audio (H-series), Security (T-series), Wireless (W-series), Ultra-Wideband (U-series), and now Spatial Computing (R-series). The acquisition of Intel’s modem business in 2019 was the final piece of this puzzle, aiming to displace Qualcomm and complete the complete silicon independence.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple’s overarching semiconductor philosophy is simple but ruthless: control the “primary technologies” that differentiate the product. This journey, which began with the A4 in 2010, has evolved into a comprehensive internalization strategy. It’s not just about the CPU (A-series/M-series); Apple has systematically replaced suppliers for almost every critical subsystem, developing custom silicon for Audio (H-series), Security (T-series), Wireless (W-series), Ultra-Wideband (U-series), and now Spatial Computing (R-series). The acquisition of Intel’s modem business in 2019 was the final piece of this puzzle, aiming to displace Qualcomm and complete the complete silicon independence.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple’s overarching semiconductor philosophy is simple but ruthless: control the “primary technologies” that differentiate the product. This journey, which began with the A4 in 2010, has evolved into a comprehensive internalization strategy. It’s not just about the CPU (A-series/M-series); Apple has systematically replaced suppliers for almost every critical subsystem, developing custom silicon for Audio (H-series), Security (T-series), Wireless (W-series), Ultra-Wideband (U-series), and now Spatial Computing (R-series). The acquisition of Intel’s modem business in 2019 was the final piece of this puzzle, aiming to displace Qualcomm and complete the complete silicon independence.

pro.text: 苹果的核心半导体理念简单却展现出极致的掌控欲：掌控那些能让产品脱颖而出的“核心技术”。这一进程始于 2010 年的 A4 芯片，如今已演变为全面的内部化战略。这绝不仅仅局限于 CPU（A系列/M系列）；苹果已系统性地替换了几乎所有关键子系统的外部供应商，为音频（H系列）、安全（T系列）、无线通信（W系列）、超宽带（U系列）乃至如今的空间计算（R系列）开发定制芯片。2019 年收购英特尔基带业务是这块拼图的最后一块，意在取代高通，彻底实现全面的芯片独立。

applied_terms: Translate 'virtual IDM' as 虚拟IDM or 虚拟垂直整合制造模式.
Translate 'Process Design Kit (PDK)' as 工艺设计套件 (PDK).
Capture the intensity of 'ruthless' in a business context (e.g., 极其冷酷/极致的掌控欲).

understanding.role_in_article: Details Apple's aggressive M&A strategy to internalize silicon design, culminating in a 'virtual IDM' relationship with TSMC that dictates process design.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Pivots from external manufacturing risks to internal design control, showing how Apple mitigates risk by owning the underlying IP.
understanding.relation_to_next: Establishes Apple's deep integration at the PDK level, setting up the final technical outlook on future node optimizations in the concluding section.
key_points: Apple's philosophy is to ruthlessly control 'primary technologies' across all critical subsystems (Audio, Security, Wireless, UWB).
The $1B Intel modem acquisition was a pivotal step toward full in-house connectivity silicon.
Apple and TSMC operate as a 'virtual IDM', with TSMC dedicating hundreds of engineers to customize the Process Design Kit (PDK) specifically for Apple.

paragraphs.source: Apple bought Intel’s smartphone modem business the same week they settled their lawsuit with Qualcomm. The timing was intentional. The settlement was a temporary truce.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple bought Intel’s smartphone modem business the same week they settled their lawsuit with Qualcomm. The timing was intentional. The settlement was a temporary truce.

paragraphs.source: Apple co-defines the Process Design Kit (PDK) with TSMC. TSMC effectively dedicates hundreds of engineers to Apple, creating what is essentially a “virtual IDM” (Integrated Device Manufacturer). When Apple dictates a need for wider memory buses or specific transistor architectures, TSMC adjusts the PDK to match.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple co-defines the Process Design Kit (PDK) with TSMC. TSMC effectively dedicates hundreds of engineers to Apple, creating what is essentially a “virtual IDM” (Integrated Device Manufacturer). When Apple dictates a need for wider memory buses or specific transistor architectures, TSMC adjusts the PDK to match.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple co-defines the Process Design Kit (PDK) with TSMC. TSMC effectively dedicates hundreds of engineers to Apple, creating what is essentially a “virtual IDM” (Integrated Device Manufacturer). When Apple dictates a need for wider memory buses or specific transistor architectures, TSMC adjusts the PDK to match.

pro.text: 苹果与台积电共同定义了工艺设计套件（PDK）。台积电实质上为苹果配备了数百名专属工程师，打造出一种本质上的“虚拟IDM”（垂直整合制造商）模式。当苹果提出需要更宽的内存总线或特定的晶体管架构时，台积电便会相应调整 PDK 予以配合。

paragraphs.source: The result of this manufacturing dominance is a performance lead that has compounded over a decade. Apple’s ability to move to the next node first allows it to pack more transistors into the same thermal envelope, maintaining a performance-per-watt lead over the x86 ecosystem.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The result of this manufacturing dominance is a performance lead that has compounded over a decade. Apple’s ability to move to the next node first allows it to pack more transistors into the same thermal envelope, maintaining a performance-per-watt lead over the x86 ecosystem.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The result of this manufacturing dominance is a performance lead that has compounded over a decade. Apple’s ability to move to the next node first allows it to pack more transistors into the same thermal envelope, maintaining a performance-per-watt lead over the x86 ecosystem.

pro.text: 这种制造领域的统治力，造就了苹果过去十年间如复利般累积的性能领先优势。凭借率先迈向下一代制程节点的能力，苹果得以在相同的散热包络内容纳更多晶体管，在每瓦性能上持续压制 x86 生态系统。

issues.description: 译文“8宽解码”中，阿拉伯数字与中文字符之间缺少空格，不符合中英文混合排版规范（CJK-Latin spacing）。
译文“均热板与 A19 Pro 的散热包络系协同设计而成”中的“系”字带有较强的公文或法务色彩，在硬核科技分析文章中略显生硬。

paragraphs.source: The transistor count growth is linear, but performance per watt is the real metric. The shift to N3E in the A18/M4 generation prioritized cost and yield over pure density scaling (compared to N3B), which explains the relatively modest transistor count jumps compared to previous generations. The Neural Engine has seen the most exponential growth, jumping from 0.6 TOPS in A11 to 35 TOPS in A17/A18, reflecting Apple’s strategic pivot to on-device AI years before the “AI PC” hype cycle began.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The transistor count growth is linear, but performance per watt is the real metric. The shift to N3E in the A18/M4 generation prioritized cost and yield over pure density scaling (compared to N3B), which explains the relatively modest transistor count jumps compared to previous generations. The Neural Engine has seen the most exponential growth, jumping from 0.6 TOPS in A11 to 35 TOPS in A17/A18, reflecting Apple’s strategic pivot to on-device AI years before the “AI PC” hype cycle began.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The transistor count growth is linear, but performance per watt is the real metric. The shift to N3E in the A18/M4 generation prioritized cost and yield over pure density scaling (compared to N3B), which explains the relatively modest transistor count jumps compared to previous generations. The Neural Engine has seen the most exponential growth, jumping from 0.6 TOPS in A11 to 35 TOPS in A17/A18, reflecting Apple’s strategic pivot to on-device AI years before the “AI PC” hype cycle began.

pro.text: 晶体管数量呈线性增长，但每瓦性能才是核心指标。在 A18/M4 世代向 N3E 节点的过渡中，苹果优先考量成本与良率，而非纯粹的晶体管密度微缩（相比 N3B），因此与前几代相比，其晶体管数量的增幅相对温和。神经网络引擎则经历了最惊人的指数级爆发，算力从 A11 的 0.6 TOPS 一路狂飙至 A17/A18 的 35 TOPS。这印证了苹果早在“AI PC”炒作周期爆发的数年前，就已将战略重心全面转向端侧 AI。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s philosophy: massive fast L1, large shared L2, and a System-Level Cache (SLC) before DRAM. The SLC allows CPU, GPU, and Neural Engine to share data without hitting slow system memory.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple’s philosophy: massive fast L1, large shared L2, and a System-Level Cache (SLC) before DRAM. The SLC allows CPU, GPU, and Neural Engine to share data without hitting slow system memory.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple’s philosophy: massive fast L1, large shared L2, and a System-Level Cache (SLC) before DRAM. The SLC allows CPU, GPU, and Neural Engine to share data without hitting slow system memory.

pro.text: 苹果的设计理念是：配备超大高速的 L1 缓存、大容量共享的 L2 缓存，并在 DRAM 之前设置系统级缓存 (SLC)。SLC 使得 CPU、GPU 和神经网络引擎能够直接共享数据，无需调用缓慢的系统内存。

paragraphs.source: Traditional PC architectures have separate memory pools for CPU and GPU, requiring data copies. Apple Silicon uses Unified Memory Architecture: the GPU reads the exact same memory address as the CPU.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Traditional PC architectures have separate memory pools for CPU and GPU, requiring data copies. Apple Silicon uses Unified Memory Architecture: the GPU reads the exact same memory address as the CPU.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Traditional PC architectures have separate memory pools for CPU and GPU, requiring data copies. Apple Silicon uses Unified Memory Architecture: the GPU reads the exact same memory address as the CPU.

pro.text: 传统 PC 架构为 CPU 和 GPU 配备独立的内存池，需要进行数据复制。苹果自研芯片采用统一内存架构：GPU 读取的内存地址与 CPU 完全一致。

paragraphs.source: This eliminates the “copy penalty” critical for AI workloads. A 20GB LLM loads once. The Neural Engine and GPU access it simultaneously. Qualcomm mobile chips use shared LPDDR5X but lack Apple’s fine-grained coherency. AMD Strix Halo’s MALL is GPU-only.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: This eliminates the “copy penalty” critical for AI workloads. A 20GB LLM loads once. The Neural Engine and GPU access it simultaneously. Qualcomm mobile chips use shared LPDDR5X but lack Apple’s fine-grained coherency. AMD Strix Halo’s MALL is GPU-only.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: This eliminates the “copy penalty” critical for AI workloads. A 20GB LLM loads once. The Neural Engine and GPU access it simultaneously. Qualcomm mobile chips use shared LPDDR5X but lack Apple’s fine-grained coherency. AMD Strix Halo’s MALL is GPU-only.

pro.text: 这消除了对 AI 负载至关重要的“复制惩罚”。一个 20GB 的 LLM 只需加载一次，神经网络引擎与 GPU 即可同时访问。高通移动芯片虽使用共享的 LPDDR5X，但缺乏苹果的细粒度一致性。AMD Strix Halo 的 MALL (内存附加末级缓存) 则仅供 GPU 使用。

pro.text:
1. 解码能力追平：英特尔、AMD 和高通均在 2024 年实现 8 宽解码架构。
2. 引入 SLC：高通新增 8MB SLC；英特尔新增 8MB 内存侧缓存。
3. L1 缓存追平：高通 Oryon 核心配备与苹果同等的 320KB L1 缓存，且延迟相近。
4. 跑分逼近：安卓阵营的基准测试成绩正不断逼近 iPhone。
5. 深度定制：小米自研 XRing 芯片在设计、功耗和软件层面实现了深度的垂直整合。

paragraphs.source: Next we’ll dive further into specifics: Apple wafer demand and economics at TSMC, including our forecasts through the end of the decade at the A14 node. We’ll quantify Apple N2 demand and how that affects their need for older nodes - surprisingly, som older nodes are seeing increased demand from Apple. More numbers are shown for demand by chip and you’ll see the change as the company (tried to) diversify beyond the iPhone. We’ll also put numbers on the HPC phenomena as, discussed earlier, Nvidia demand encroaches Apple at the leading edge.

pro.text: 接下来我们将深入剖析具体细节：苹果在台积电的晶圆需求与经济账，涵盖我们对台积电 A14 节点（译注：指台积电 1.4 纳米节点，而非苹果处理器）直至本年代末的预测。我们将量化苹果的 N2 需求，并分析其如何影响旧制程节点需求——令人意外的是，苹果对部分旧节点的需求不降反升。文中将展示按芯片划分的详细需求数据，揭示苹果试图打破 iPhone 单一依赖所带来的转变。正如前文所述，英伟达的需求正在先进制程节点上挤压苹果的份额，我们也将用具体数据来量化这一 HPC 爆发态势。

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。
“went sideways”在金融或商业图表中意为“持平、没有明显增长或下降”。译为“横盘震荡”多了一层“上下波动（震荡）”的含义。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s wafer demand at TSMC grew 3.2x between 2014 and 2025, translating to an 11% CAGR. But that topline figure obscures the actual journey. The growth was a staircase.

paragraphs.source: From 2014 to 2017, demand grew steadily at around 10% annually as 16nm and 20nm ramped alongside iPhone volumes. Then growth stalled. Between 2018 and 2019, total wafer demand went sideways even as N7 entered production. Apple was digesting the prior node transition before the next leg could begin.

pro.text: 2014 至 2017 年间，随着 16nm 和 20nm 伴随 iPhone 销量爬坡，需求以每年约 10% 的速度稳步增长。随后增长停滞。2018 至 2019 年间，尽管 N7 进入量产阶段，总晶圆需求却横盘整理。苹果正在消化上一次节点转换，蓄力开启下一轮增长。

paragraphs.source: The real inflection came in 2020. Demand surged 37% in a single year as N5 ramped and Apple Silicon launched for Mac. The following two years sustained that momentum, with demand nearly doubling between 2019 and 2022. This was the steepest slope in the entire dataset, and it fundamentally changed Apple’s silicon footprint.

pro.text: 真正的拐点出现在 2020 年。随着 N5 产能爬坡以及 Mac 搭载苹果自研芯片发布，晶圆需求在单年内飙升 37%。随后两年延续了这一强劲势头，2019 至 2022 年间需求几乎翻倍。这是整个数据集中最陡峭的增长曲线，并从根本上重塑了苹果的芯片版图。

paragraphs.source: Then came deceleration. In 2024, Apple posted its first year-over-year volume decline since 2013. N5 collapsed faster than N3 could absorb, and total demand contracted roughly 10%. But 2025 marks a sharp rebound as N3 peaks and N2 enters production.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Then came deceleration. In 2024, Apple posted its first year-over-year volume decline since 2013. N5 collapsed faster than N3 could absorb, and total demand contracted roughly 10%. But 2025 marks a sharp rebound as N3 peaks and N2 enters production.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Then came deceleration. In 2024, Apple posted its first year-over-year volume decline since 2013. N5 collapsed faster than N3 could absorb, and total demand contracted roughly 10%. But 2025 marks a sharp rebound as N3 peaks and N2 enters production.

pro.text: 紧接着便是增速放缓。2024 年，苹果录得自 2013 年以来的首次需求同比下滑。N5 需求崩盘的速度超过 N3 的承接能力，导致总需求萎缩约 10%。但随着 N3 达到峰值且 N2 投入量产，2025 年将迎来强势反弹。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s share of each new node at launch tells a striking story. On 20nm in 2014, Apple drove 77% of node revenue at peak. On 16nm in 2016, the peak was 46%. On N7 in 2018, it was 47%. Then N5 arrived, and Apple captured 75% of node revenue in 2021. On N3 in 2023, Apple’s share hit 92%.

On N7 in 2018, it was 47%. Then N5 arrived, and Apple captured 75% of node revenue in 2021. On N3 in 2023, Apple’s share hit 92%.

pro.text: 苹果在每个新节点发布初期的份额占比，揭示了一个惊人的趋势。2014 年在 20nm 节点上，苹果在巅峰期贡献了该节点 77% 的营收。2016 年在 16nm 节点上，这一峰值为 46%。2018 年在 N7 节点上为 47%。随后 N5 问世，苹果在 2021 年攫取了该节点 75% 的营收。到了 2023 年的 N3 节点，苹果的份额更是高达 92%。

paragraphs.source: That last number is extraordinary. Apple funded nearly the entire yield learning curve for TSMC’s most advanced node. By the time other customers reached volume, Apple had already extracted the premium economics. The trend line is convex: Apple is capturing progressively larger shares of each new node at launch.

pro.text: 最后一个数字堪称不可思议。苹果几乎以一己之力，承担了台积电最先进制程节点整个良率学习曲线的资金成本。等到其他客户实现规模量产时，苹果早已充分榨取了丰厚的早期经济红利。这一趋势线呈凸型增长（前置爆发）：在每个新节点发布初期，苹果攫取的份额正持续攀升。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s share of total TSMC revenue moved within a band of 17% to 24% over the decade. Apple’s share spikes when it leads a new node ramp, then fades as other customers scale, then spikes again at the next transition.

pro.text: 过去十年间，苹果占台积电总营收的份额一直在 17% 至 24% 的区间内波动。当苹果主导新节点产能爬坡时，其份额会急剧飙升；随着其他客户扩大规模，该份额随之回落；到了下一次节点转换时，份额又会再次飙升。

paragraphs.source: The 2023 peak of 24% coincided with N3’s launch year. The 2025 trough of 17% reflects AI accelerator customers absorbing leading-edge capacity. NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscaler ASICs grew faster than Apple during this window. Apple’s absolute spend continued rising, but its relative share shrank because TSMC’s total revenue grew even faster.

paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The 2023 peak of 24% coincided with N3’s launch year. The 2025 trough of 17% reflects AI accelerator customers absorbing leading-edge capacity. NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscaler ASICs grew faster than Apple during this window. Apple’s absolute spend continued rising, but its relative share shrank because TSMC’s total revenue grew even faster.

pro.text: 2023 年 24% 的峰值与 N3 的发布年份重合。2025 年 17% 的谷值则反映出 AI 加速器客户正在吸纳先进制程产能。在此期间，英伟达、AMD 和超大规模云服务商的 ASIC (专用集成电路) 增速超越了苹果。尽管苹果的绝对支出仍在持续攀升，但由于台积电总营收增速更快，其相对份额随之缩水。

paragraphs.source: If we remove the 2020 outlier from the CAGR calculation, the underlying growth rate drops from 11% to closer to 8%. That single year, driven by N5 ramp plus COVID-fuelled Apple Silicon demand, inflates the topline number. While the growth trend is there, the magnitude is overstated by one exceptional year.

pro.text: 如果我们在计算复合年增长率（CAGR）时剔除 2020 年这一异常值，实际底层增长率将从 11% 降至接近 8%。在 N5 产能爬坡以及疫情激发的苹果自研芯片需求双重驱动下，单单这一年就拉高了整体增长数据。尽管增长趋势依然存在，但这一特殊年份夸大了实际的增长幅度。

paragraphs.source: N2 marks a structural shift in TSMC’s customer mix at node launch. We estimate Apple will once again the table with high share in N2 in 2026, but that represents <50% of N2 demand. Qualcomm (Snapdragon 8 Elite 2), AMD (Venice and MI450x), MediaTek (Dimensity 9600), Broadcom (ASIC), and Intel (Nova Lake) will follow. This is unprecedented. Previous leading-edge nodes were mobile-heavy, with Apple capturing >90% at peak. N2 splits demand across smartphones, PCs, servers, and networking from day one. HPC now drives capacity allocation, and Apple faces real competition for leading-edge capacity for the first time in a decade.

sections.title: Apple’s Wafer Demand Model: Forecast Analysis (2026-2030)
sections.title_translation: 苹果晶圆需求模型：预测分析（2026-2030）

paragraphs.source: N2 Breaks the Pattern
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: ### N2 Breaks the Pattern
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: N2 Breaks the Pattern

pro.text: N2 打破既定格局

issues.description: 译文“台积电份额回升”存在歧义，容易被误解为“台积电的市场份额回升”。根据下文“Apple’s share of total TSMC revenue will likely recover”，这里指的是苹果在台积电总营收中所占的份额回升。
“已让客户结构发生了结构性转变”中的“让……发生了”略显口语化，且“发生”与“结构性转变”搭配稍显冗长。
“N系列”、“C系列”、“AI加速器”等词汇中，英文字母/缩写与中文字符之间缺少标准的中英文字符间距（半角空格）。该问题在第2、4、7、14、15、16段中多次出现。
译文“还尚未问世”存在轻微的语义重复，“还”和“尚未”在表达“未发生”时功能重叠。
“每月晶圆投片量（wspm）”翻译正确但略显生硬，wspm (wafers starts per month) 在半导体行业中文里有更标准的惯用表述。

paragraphs.source: N2 enters production in 2025 with 234K wafers and scales rapidly to 778K by 2027. The volume trajectory looks familiar. But Apple’s share tells a different story.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: N2 enters production in 2025 with 234K wafers and scales rapidly to 778K by 2027. The volume trajectory looks familiar. But Apple’s share tells a different story.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: N2 enters production in 2025 with 234K wafers and scales rapidly to 778K by 2027. The volume trajectory looks familiar. But Apple’s share tells a different story.

pro.text: N2 将于 2025 年投入量产，初始产能为 23.4 万片晶圆，到 2027 年将迅速攀升至 77.8 万片。这一产能轨迹似曾相识。然而，苹果的份额却呈现出截然不同的景象。

paragraphs.source: At peak, Apple takes 48% of N2 revenue. That is down from 75% on N5 and 92% on N3. For the first time in a decade, Apple is not the dominant customer on the newest leading-edge node. AI accelerator demand from hyperscalers has structurally shifted the customer mix.

pro.text: 在峰值时期，苹果占据了 N2 营收的 48%。这一比例低于 N5 的 75% 和 N3 的 92%。十年来，苹果首次不再是最新先进制程节点上的主导客户。来自超大规模云厂商的 AI加速器需求已从结构上重塑了客户组合。

pro.text: N3 需求急速衰退

“每月晶圆投片量（wspm）”翻译正确但略显生硬，wspm (wafers starts per month) 在半导体行业中文里有更标准的惯用表述。

paragraphs.source: N3 peaks at 639K wafers in 2025, falls to 357K by 2027, and drops to 141K by 2030. That is a 78% decline in five years, faster than any prior node decay. Apple is not keeping N3 alive for legacy products the way it sustained older nodes. Once the transition begins, the exit is rapid. This is mostly due transition to N2 and Apple’a vacated capacity will be picked up by AI accelerators. TSMC, most recently, even expanded N3 capacity by 30-40k wspm to accommodate incoming accelerator demand.

This is mostly due transition to N2 and Apple’a vacated capacity will be picked up by AI accelerators. TSMC, most recently, even expanded N3 capacity by 30-40k wspm to accommodate incoming accelerator demand.

TSMC, most recently, even expanded N3 capacity by 30-40k wspm to accommodate incoming accelerator demand.

pro.text: N3 晶圆需求在 2025 年达到 63.9 万片的峰值，到 2027 年降至 35.7 万片，并于 2030 年一路陡降至 14.1 万片。五年内降幅高达 78%，衰退速度超过以往任何制程节点。苹果并未像维持旧制程节点那样，为了旧款产品而保留 N3 产能。一旦开始过渡，撤出速度极快。这主要归因于向 N2 的过渡，而苹果腾出的产能将被 AI加速器接盘。台积电近期甚至扩充了 3 万至 4 万片晶圆月投片量（wspm）的 N3 产能，以满足涌入的加速器需求。

paragraphs.source: N5 is the first Apple node to show a U-shaped recovery. It bottoms at 157K wafers in 2025, then climbs back to 434K by 2030. Prior nodes decayed monotonically; N5 rebounds.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: N5 is the first Apple node to show a U-shaped recovery. It bottoms at 157K wafers in 2025, then climbs back to 434K by 2030. Prior nodes decayed monotonically; N5 rebounds.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: N5 is the first Apple node to show a U-shaped recovery. It bottoms at 157K wafers in 2025, then climbs back to 434K by 2030. Prior nodes decayed monotonically; N5 rebounds.

pro.text: N5 是首个呈现 U 型复苏的苹果制程节点。其晶圆需求在 2025 年触底至 15.7 万片，随后在 2030 年攀升回 43.4 万片。以往的制程节点均呈单调衰退；N5 却迎来了反弹。

paragraphs.source: The likely explanation is cost optimization. Apple is repurposing mature N5 capacity for lower-ASP products: entry-level Macs, older iPhone variants, modem and connectivity chips (C-series/N-series), RF chips, and high-volume accessories. Revenue grows slower than volume on this node, confirming the ASP decline.

Apple is repurposing mature N5 capacity for lower-ASP products: entry-level Macs, older iPhone variants, modem and connectivity chips (C-series/N-series), RF chips, and high-volume accessories. Revenue grows slower than volume on this node, confirming the ASP decline.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The likely explanation is cost optimization. Revenue grows slower than volume on this node, confirming the ASP decline.

pro.text: 这很可能是出于成本优化的考量。苹果正将成熟的 N5 产能重新用于平均售价（ASP）较低的产品：入门级 Mac、旧款 iPhone、调制解调器与连接芯片（C系列/N系列）、射频芯片以及大批量生产的配件。该制程节点上的营收增速低于晶圆量增速，印证了 ASP 的下降。

pro.text: 1.4nm 晶圆需求在 2029 年起步时为 30.7 万片，到 2030 年暴涨至 80.8 万片。这一数字超越了 N5 历史峰值的 68.9 万片。其产能爬坡曲线是整个数据集中最陡峭的，首年产量甚至超过了 N3 的次年产量。

paragraphs.source: N-series ramps 2.5x in its first year, jumping from 4.5K to 11.4K monthly wafers. Then it nearly flatlines for four years. The profile suggests a defined product category, likely Apple’s neural engine or dedicated AI accelerator, that scales with device attach rates rather than representing a new growth vector in itself.

pro.text: N系列在首年实现 2.5 倍的产能爬坡，月度晶圆需求从 4500 片跃升至 1.14 万片。随后四年则几乎走平。这种曲线特征表明它属于特定产品类别，很可能是苹果的神经网络引擎或专用 AI加速器。其需求随设备搭载率同步增长，而非自身代表新的增长引擎。

paragraphs.source: C-series shows a different shape. It grows 6x from 2025 to 2028 (3.1K to 17.0K monthly), then plateaus. The curve is concave: growth decelerates each year. This matches a new product launch curve where early adopters drive initial volumes, then growth slows as the market matures. Vision Pro and spatial computing fit this profile.

pro.text: C系列则呈现出不同的形态。从 2025 年到 2028 年，其需求增长 6 倍（月度晶圆从 3100 片增至 1.7 万片），随后进入平台期。这是一条凹型增长（增速递减）曲线：增速逐年放缓。这符合新产品的发布曲线：早期采用者推高初始销量，随后随着市场成熟，增长逐渐放缓。Vision Pro 与空间计算正符合这一特征。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s wafer demand at TSMC grew triple digits annually from 2008-2011 as iPhone and iPad scaled. Growth moderated to single digits by 2022 and turned negative in 2023-2024 as device shipments plateaued. The 2020 spike of 37% marked the last major inflection, driven by 5G iPhone and M1 Mac launches. We project 9% wafer growth for Apple from 2025-2030.

pro.text: 2008 年至 2011 年间，随着 iPhone 和 iPad 规模扩张，苹果对台积电的晶圆需求实现连年三位数增长。到 2022 年，由于终端设备出货量步入平台期，增速回落至个位数，并在 2023 至 2024 年转为负增长。2020 年高达 37% 的激增是上一次重大拐点，主要由 5G iPhone 和 M1 Mac 的发布双重驱动。我们预计，2025 至 2030 年间苹果的晶圆需求将保持 9% 的增长。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s annual foundry spend at TSMC grew from $2.3 billion in 2014 to nearly $75 billion by 2030, a 32x increase in 16 years. The only down year was 2023, when revenue dipped 1.3% as the N5-to-N3 transition caused a temporary node gap. Despite this growth, Apple’s share of TSMC’s total revenue peaked at 25.5% in 2021 and has since oscillated between 19% and 26%, a reminder that Apple’s spend is growing but TSMC’s AI-driven revenue is growing just as fast.

pro.text: 苹果在台积电的年度晶圆代工支出从 2014 年的 23 亿美元飙升至 2030 年的近 750 亿美元，16 年间狂飙 32 倍。唯一下滑的年份是 2023 年，当时 N5 向 N3 过渡引发了暂时的制程节点空档期，导致营收微降 1.3%。尽管支出规模持续扩张，苹果占台积电总营收的份额在 2021 年触及 25.5% 的历史高点后，便一直在 19% 至 26% 之间震荡。这表明，尽管苹果的支出在不断攀升，但台积电由 AI 驱动的营收增速同样迅猛。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s annual wafer demand at TSMC more than quadrupled between 2014 and 2030. Growth was not linear: it came in waves aligned with node transitions, with the steepest jumps in 2020-2021 (25% and 23% respectively) as N5 and Apple Silicon ramped simultaneously. The only back-to-back negative years in the dataset were 2023-2024, when demand contracted as the N5-to-N3 transition created a temporary gap. The 2026 rebound of over 17% signals the start of the next growth phase as N2 scales and new chip families reach volume.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple’s annual wafer demand at TSMC more than quadrupled between 2014 and 2030. Growth was not linear: it came in waves aligned with node transitions, with the steepest jumps in 2020-2021 (25% and 23% respectively) as N5 and Apple Silicon ramped simultaneously. The only back-to-back negative years in the dataset were 2023-2024, when demand contracted as the N5-to-N3 transition created a temporary gap. The 2026 rebound of over 17% signals the start of the next growth phase as N2 scales and new chip families reach volume.

The 2026 rebound of over 17% signals the start of the next growth phase as N2 scales and new chip families reach volume.

pro.text: 2014 年至 2030 年间，苹果对台积电的年度晶圆需求量翻了四倍有余。这种增长并非线性，而是与制程节点迭代同频共振，呈波浪式推进。最陡峭的跃升出现在 2020 至 2021 年（分别增长 25% 和 23%），当时 N5 与苹果自研芯片同步开启产能爬坡。数据集中仅有的连续负增长出现在 2023 至 2024 年，当时 N5 向 N3 过渡造成了暂时的空档期，导致需求收缩。2026 年超 17% 的反弹则标志着新一轮增长周期的开启，届时 N2 将实现规模化量产，全新芯片系列也将开始放量。

paragraphs.source: TSMC's A16 (N2) is architected for HPC workloads. Backside power delivery, gate-all-around transistors, and thermal envelopes are optimized for data center power budgets, not smartphone battery constraints. Nvidia benefits disproportionately. Apple may adopt A16 for the future A21/M6 Pro generation, but the node's design tradeoffs favor Nvidia's use case over Apple's. This is why Apple's share drops to 48% on N2. Apple did not lose leverage. The node was built for a different customer.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: TSMC's A16 (N2) is architected for HPC workloads. Backside power delivery, gate-all-around transistors, and thermal envelopes are optimized for data center power budgets, not smartphone battery constraints. Nvidia benefits disproportionately. Apple may adopt A16 for the future A21/M6 Pro generation, but the node's design tradeoffs favor Nvidia's use case over Apple's. This is why Apple's share drops to 48% on N2. Apple did not lose leverage. The node was built for a different customer.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: TSMC's A16 (N2) is architected for HPC workloads. Backside power delivery, gate-all-around transistors, and thermal envelopes are optimized for data center power budgets, not smartphone battery constraints. Nvidia benefits disproportionately. Apple may adopt A16 for the future A21/M6 Pro generation, but the node's design tradeoffs favor Nvidia's use case over Apple's. This is why Apple's share drops to 48% on N2. Apple did not lose leverage. The node was built for a different customer.

pro.text: 台积电的 A16（N2）制程节点专为 HPC 工作负载而设计。背面供电、环绕栅极晶体管以及热设计边界均针对数据中心功耗预算量身定制，而非迁就智能手机的电池瓶颈。英伟达成为最大受益者。苹果可能会在未来的 A21/M6 Pro 芯片世代中采用 A16 节点，但该制程节点的设计取舍更契合英伟达的应用场景，而非苹果。这正是苹果在 N2 节点上的份额跌至 48% 的原因。苹果并未丧失话语权。只是该制程节点本就为另一类客户而生。

paragraphs.source: A14 (1.4nm) rebalances the equation. TSMC is designing A14 for both mobile and HPC from the start, with the HPC-focused A14 SPR coming after the mobile-oriented A14 base process. Power efficiency curves favor consumer devices again. Our model shows Apple reclaims 67% node share on A14, its highest since N3. The timing aligns with Apple’s product cadence: A23 Pro and M8 in 2029-2030.

pro.text: A14（1.4nm）制程节点重新平衡了这一局面。台积电从一开始就将 A14 节点设计为兼顾移动端与 HPC，主打移动端的 A14 基础工艺率先登场，随后才是主攻 HPC 的 A14 SPR。能效曲线再次向消费级设备倾斜。我们的模型显示，苹果在 A14 节点上收复了 67% 的制程节点份额，创下自 N3 以来的新高。这一时间点与苹果的产品节奏完美契合：即 2029 至 2030 年间推出的 A23 Pro 和 M8 芯片。

pro.text: 市场共识将苹果在 N2 节点上的份额下滑解读为永久性失势。但制程节点路线图给出了不同的答案。A16 是英伟达的制程节点（译注：这里的 A16 指台积电的 1.6 纳米埃米级节点，而非苹果处理器）。A14 则是苹果的王者归来。

issues.description: 译文“台积电份额回升”存在歧义，容易被误解为“台积电的市场份额回升”。根据下文“Apple’s share of total TSMC revenue will likely recover”，这里指的是苹果在台积电总营收中所占的份额回升。
“已让客户结构发生了结构性转变”中的“让……发生了”略显口语化，且“发生”与“结构性转变”搭配稍显冗长。
“N系列”、“C系列”、“AI加速器”等词汇中，英文字母/缩写与中文字符之间缺少标准的中英文字符间距（半角空格）。该问题在第2、4、7、14、15、16段中多次出现。
译文“还尚未问世”存在轻微的语义重复，“还”和“尚未”在表达“未发生”时功能重叠。
“每月晶圆投片量（wspm）”翻译正确但略显生硬，wspm (wafers starts per month) 在半导体行业中文里有更标准的惯用表述。

pro.text: 出货量脱钩

issues.description: 原文“2020 to 2030”结合过去时“grew”明显为笔误，大概率应为“2023”。译文直接照译为“2030 年”会导致事实逻辑上的时间冲突。
中英文之间缺少必要的空格（A系列、M系列）。
“解释了，为何”中间的逗号打断了语流。
“已实现显著的多元化”使用了“实现……的……”这种抽象名词外壳，有轻微翻译腔。

paragraphs.source: The decoupling between unit shipments and silicon value tells the real story of Apple’s foundry economics. A-series unit growth has essentially flatlined since 2017, yet A-series wafer value has nearly tripled over the same period. Each iPhone now generates roughly 3x the foundry revenue it did seven years ago, driven by larger dies, more expensive nodes, and expanding silicon content beyond just the main SoC. M-series shows the same pattern in compressed form: units grew 72x from 2020 to 2030, but value grew 222x. Apple is shipping exponentially more silicon per device. This decoupling explains why Apple’s absolute TSMC spend continues rising even as iPhone unit growth stalls and Mac volumes remain modest. The Apple-TSMC story shifted from units to silicon content intensity per unit.

A-series unit growth has essentially flatlined since 2017, yet A-series wafer value has nearly tripled over the same period. M-series shows the same pattern in compressed form: units grew 72x from 2020 to 2030, but value grew 222x. Apple is shipping exponentially more silicon per device. This decoupling explains why Apple’s absolute TSMC spend continues rising even as iPhone unit growth stalls and Mac volumes remain modest. The Apple-TSMC story shifted from units to silicon content intensity per unit.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The decoupling between unit shipments and silicon value tells the real story of Apple’s foundry economics. A-series unit growth has essentially flatlined since 2017, yet A-series wafer value has nearly tripled over the same period. Each iPhone now generates roughly 3x the foundry revenue it did seven years ago, driven by larger dies, more expensive nodes, and expanding silicon content beyond just the main SoC. M-series shows the same pattern in compressed form: units grew 72x from 2020 to 2030, but value grew 222x. Apple is shipping exponentially more silicon per device. This decoupling explains why Apple’s absolute TSMC spend continues rising even as iPhone unit growth stalls and Mac volumes remain modest. The Apple-TSMC story shifted from units to silicon content intensity per unit.

pro.text: 出货量与芯片价值的脱钩，揭示了苹果晶圆代工经济学的真实面貌。自 2017 年以来，A系列出货量增长已基本停滞，但同期 A系列晶圆价值却增至近三倍。在更大尺寸的裸片、更昂贵的制程节点，以及主 SoC 之外不断扩张的硅含量的多重驱动下，如今每部 iPhone 创造的代工厂营收大约是七年前的三倍。M系列在更短的时间内展现了相同的规律：从 2020 年到 2030 年，其出货量增长 72 倍，价值却暴增 222 倍。苹果单台设备的硅含量正呈指数级飙升。这种脱钩现象解释了，为何在 iPhone 出货量增长停滞、Mac 销量保持平稳的情况下，苹果在台积电的绝对支出仍在持续攀升。苹果与台积电的合作主线，已从单纯的出货量，转向单台设备的硅含量强度。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s wafer demand at TSMC has diversified meaningfully since 2018. iPhone accounted for 74% of Apple’s monthly wafer consumption that year. By 2025, that share dropped to 57%. Mac silicon, nonexistent before M1, now represents 12% of the mix. The “Other” category, which includes mature node chips, doubled its share from 7% to 14%. iPhone remains the anchor, but Mac, wearables, and custom chips have become meaningful contributors. TSMC now serves five Apple product lines at scale, reducing revenue volatility tied to iPhone cycles.

pro.text: 自 2018 年以来，苹果在台积电的晶圆需求已实现显著的多元化。当年，iPhone 占苹果每月晶圆消耗量的 74%。到 2025 年，这一比例降至 57%。在 M1 之前根本不存在的 Mac 芯片，如今在整体份额中占比达 12%。包含成熟制程芯片的“其他”类别，其份额从 7% 翻倍至 14%。iPhone 依然是基石，但 Mac、可穿戴设备以及定制芯片已成为重要的贡献者。台积电如今大规模服务于苹果的五大产品线，有效降低了与 iPhone 周期绑定的营收波动。
