paragraphs.source: The device base is massive but mature. Apple now ships over 600 million devices annually across all categories. But growth rates tell a different story: the 20%+ annual growth of 2020-2021 has compressed to around 3% through the forecast period. The portfolio is collectively plateauing.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The device base is massive but mature. Apple now ships over 600 million devices annually across all categories. But growth rates tell a different story: the 20%+ annual growth of 2020-2021 has compressed to around 3% through the forecast period. The portfolio is collectively plateauing.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The device base is massive but mature. Apple now ships over 600 million devices annually across all categories. But growth rates tell a different story: the 20%+ annual growth of 2020-2021 has compressed to around 3% through the forecast period. The portfolio is collectively plateauing.

pro.text: 设备基数庞大但已趋于成熟。苹果目前全品类设备年出货量已超 6 亿台。但增长率却揭示了另一番景象：2020 至 2021 年间超 20% 的年增长率，在预测期内已压缩至 3% 左右。整体产品组合正步入平台期。

sections.title: AI Overtakes Apple at the Leading Edge
sections.title_translation: AI在先进制程上超越苹果

paragraphs.source: For most of TSMC’s advanced node history, Apple was the undisputed volume leader. On N5, Apple’s quarterly wafer demand consistently exceeded the combined demand from NVIDIA, Broadcom, Amazon, and AMD through 2023. That hierarchy has now inverted. By 4Q25, NVIDIA alone consumed more N5 wafers than Apple’s entire A-series and M-series combined. The gap widened on N3. Apple ramped N3 first, taking 92% of node capacity in 2023, but AI accelerators followed with overwhelming force. By 2Q26, AI ecosystem N3 demand (led by NVIDIA’s Blackwell successors) exceeds Apple’s N3 demand by nearly 3x. The crossover happened in under 18 months.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: For most of TSMC’s advanced node history, Apple was the undisputed volume leader. On N5, Apple’s quarterly wafer demand consistently exceeded the combined demand from NVIDIA, Broadcom, Amazon, and AMD through 2023. That hierarchy has now inverted. By 4Q25, NVIDIA alone consumed more N5 wafers than Apple’s entire A-series and M-series combined. The gap widened on N3. Apple ramped N3 first, taking 92% of node capacity in 2023, but AI accelerators followed with overwhelming force. By 2Q26, AI ecosystem N3 demand (led by NVIDIA’s Blackwell successors) exceeds Apple’s N3 demand by nearly 3x. The crossover happened in under 18 months.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: For most of TSMC’s advanced node history, Apple was the undisputed volume leader. On N5, Apple’s quarterly wafer demand consistently exceeded the combined demand from NVIDIA, Broadcom, Amazon, and AMD through 2023. That hierarchy has now inverted. By 4Q25, NVIDIA alone consumed more N5 wafers than Apple’s entire A-series and M-series combined. The gap widened on N3. Apple ramped N3 first, taking 92% of node capacity in 2023, but AI accelerators followed with overwhelming force. By 2Q26, AI ecosystem N3 demand (led by NVIDIA’s Blackwell successors) exceeds Apple’s N3 demand by nearly 3x. The crossover happened in under 18 months.

pro.text: 在台积电先进制程的大部分历史中，苹果是无可争议的产能霸主。在 N5 上，直到 2023 年，苹果的季度晶圆需求始终超过英伟达、博通、亚马逊和 AMD 的总和。如今，这一格局已彻底逆转。到 2025 年第四季度，仅英伟达一家消耗的 N5 晶圆，就超过了苹果所有 A系列和 M系列的总和。在 N3 上，差距进一步拉大。苹果率先推进 N3 产能爬坡，在 2023 年拿下了该制程节点 92% 的产能，但 AI 加速器随后以排山倒海之势席卷而来。到 2026 年第二季度，以英伟达 Blackwell 继任者为首的 AI 生态系统 N3 需求，已超出苹果 N3 需求近三倍。完成这一历史性交替仅用了不到 18 个月。

issues.description: “countervailing trend”在这里的语境是指能够抵消“苹果在台积电总营收份额下降”这一负面影响的积极趋势（即单机营收上升）。译为“与之抗衡的趋势”略带冲突意味，未能精准传达财务分析中“对冲/抵消”的含义。
“每年能力压”读起来节奏略显生硬，“能”作为助动词在此处与“力压”结合不够流畅，且削弱了“every single year”的绝对性语气。
“A系列和 M系列”中，英文字母与中文字符之间缺少空格（A与系之间缺少空格，M与系之间虽然有，但排版不一致）。第10段中的“M系列 Mac”也存在同样的问题。

paragraphs.source: The composition of AI demand makes the shift even more striking. NVIDIA alone is projected to consume more wafers on N3 by 4Q27 than Apple’s entire leading-edge portfolio across all chip families. Broadcom, Amazon, and AMD add to this tally.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The composition of AI demand makes the shift even more striking. NVIDIA alone is projected to consume more wafers on N3 by 4Q27 than Apple’s entire leading-edge portfolio across all chip families. Broadcom, Amazon, and AMD add to this tally.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The composition of AI demand makes the shift even more striking. NVIDIA alone is projected to consume more wafers on N3 by 4Q27 than Apple’s entire leading-edge portfolio across all chip families. Broadcom, Amazon, and AMD add to this tally.

pro.text: AI 需求的构成让这一转变愈发令人瞩目。预计到 2027 年第四季度，仅英伟达一家在 N3 上消耗的晶圆数量，就将超过苹果旗下所有芯片产品线的全部先进制程需求。博通、亚马逊和 AMD 更是进一步推高了这一总量。

paragraphs.source: The structural implication is clear. Apple built TSMC’s leading-edge business over the past decade, funding yield learning and absorbing startup capacity on every new node. AI customers are now using that infrastructure at scale. Apple remains TSMC’s largest smartphone customer and a critical anchor tenant, but it is no longer the defining force at the bleeding edge. TSMC’s largest growth driver is now Nvidia.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The structural implication is clear. Apple built TSMC’s leading-edge business over the past decade, funding yield learning and absorbing startup capacity on every new node. AI customers are now using that infrastructure at scale. Apple remains TSMC’s largest smartphone customer and a critical anchor tenant, but it is no longer the defining force at the bleeding edge. TSMC’s largest growth driver is now Nvidia.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The structural implication is clear. Apple built TSMC’s leading-edge business over the past decade, funding yield learning and absorbing startup capacity on every new node. AI customers are now using that infrastructure at scale. Apple remains TSMC’s largest smartphone customer and a critical anchor tenant, but it is no longer the defining force at the bleeding edge. TSMC’s largest growth driver is now Nvidia.

pro.text: 这一结构性变化释放的信号十分明确。过去十年，苹果撑起了台积电的先进制程业务，在每一个新制程节点上为良率学习买单，并消化初始产能。如今，AI 客户正在大规模使用这一基础设施。苹果依然是台积电最大的智能手机客户与关键的锚定客户，但已不再是最前沿节点的决定性力量。台积电如今最大的增长引擎是英伟达。

paragraphs.source: TSMC’s Platform Shift: HPC Overtakes Mobile
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: ### TSMC’s Platform Shift: HPC Overtakes Mobile
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: TSMC’s Platform Shift: HPC Overtakes Mobile

pro.text: 台积电的平台转移：HPC 超越移动端

issues.description: “countervailing trend”在这里的语境是指能够抵消“苹果在台积电总营收份额下降”这一负面影响的积极趋势（即单机营收上升）。译为“与之抗衡的趋势”略带冲突意味，未能精准传达财务分析中“对冲/抵消”的含义。
“每年能力压”读起来节奏略显生硬，“能”作为助动词在此处与“力压”结合不够流畅，且削弱了“every single year”的绝对性语气。
“A系列和 M系列”中，英文字母与中文字符之间缺少空格（A与系之间缺少空格，M与系之间虽然有，但排版不一致）。第10段中的“M系列 Mac”也存在同样的问题。

paragraphs.source: For most of TSMC’s history, smartphones were the anchor platform. From 2012 to 2021, smartphone revenue exceeded HPC revenue every single year, driven by Apple and Qualcomm scaling their SoCs on successive leading-edge nodes. For over a decade, Apple was the undisputed anchor tenant at TSMC’s leading edge. From 2012 to 2021, smartphones (led by Apple and Qualcomm) generated more foundry revenue than any other platform.

From 2012 to 2021, smartphone revenue exceeded HPC revenue every single year, driven by Apple and Qualcomm scaling their SoCs on successive leading-edge nodes. For over a decade, Apple was the undisputed anchor tenant at TSMC’s leading edge. From 2012 to 2021, smartphones (led by Apple and Qualcomm) generated more foundry revenue than any other platform.

pro.text: 在台积电的大部分发展史中，智能手机始终是其锚定平台。从 2012 年到 2021 年，在苹果与高通于历代先进制程节点上持续迭代 SoC 的双重驱动下，智能手机营收每年能力压 HPC 营收。十多年来，苹果一直是台积电先进制程上无可争议的锚定客户。2012 至 2021 年间，由苹果和高通领衔的智能手机业务，为台积电贡献的晶圆代工厂营收超越了任何其他平台。

paragraphs.source: This was dominance by design: Apple funded yield learning, absorbed startup capacity, and extracted the best economics before competitors could reach volume. The model worked because no other customer could match Apple’s combination of volume, predictability, and willingness to pay.

pro.text: 这种绝对优势源于精密的商业设计：苹果为良率学习买单，消化初始产能，并在竞争对手实现规模化量产前，攫取最优的经济效益。该模式之所以行之有效，是因为没有任何其他客户能像苹果一样，兼具庞大体量、高度可预测性与极强的支付意愿。

paragraphs.source: But 2022 marked a structural inflection. HPC revenue surpassed smartphones for the first time and has accelerated since, growing at nearly 22% CAGR versus 11% for smartphones. By 2025, HPC revenue is projected to exceed $71 billion, more than double smartphone revenue of $35 billion. The gap is widening. AI accelerators from NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscaler custom silicon have fundamentally reordered TSMC’s customer mix. Smartphones remain TSMC’s second-largest platform, but they are no longer the growth engine.

Smartphones remain TSMC’s second-largest platform, but they are no longer the growth engine.

pro.text: 但 2022 年标志着一个结构性拐点。HPC 营收首次超越智能手机，此后更是加速增长，复合年增长率（CAGR）近 22%，而智能手机仅为 11%。预计到 2025 年，HPC 营收将突破 710 亿美元，达到智能手机营收（350 亿美元）的两倍以上。这一差距仍在不断拉大。来自英伟达、AMD 的 AI 加速器以及超大规模云服务商的定制芯片，已从根本上重塑了台积电的客户结构。智能手机虽仍是台积电的第二大平台，却已不再是增长引擎。

paragraphs.source: This shift has direct implications for Apple. Despite being the largest smartphone customer at TSMC, Apple’s leverage has diminished as HPC customers absorb disproportionate leading-edge capacity. Apple’s share of total TSMC revenue fell from 24% in 2023 to 17% in 2025, even as Apple’s absolute spend continued rising. The countervailing trend is revenue per smartphone, which has tripled since 2012 and reached nearly $28 by 2025. Each iPhone now generates 3x the foundry revenue it did a decade ago, reflecting larger dies, more advanced nodes, and increasing silicon content (M-series Macs, AirPods, Apple Watch). Apple may be losing share of TSMC’s total output, but it is extracting more value per device than ever before.

pro.text: 这一转变对苹果产生了直接影响。尽管苹果是台积电最大的智能手机客户，但随着 HPC 客户吸纳了不成比例的先进制程产能，苹果的话语权已然削弱。即使苹果的绝对支出持续攀升，其在台积电总营收中的份额仍从 2023 年的 24% 跌至 2025 年的 17%。与之抗衡的趋势是单部智能手机营收，该指标自 2012 年以来已增至三倍，到 2025 年将接近 28 美元。如今，每部 iPhone 创造的晶圆代工厂营收是十年前的三倍，这反映出裸片尺寸更大、制程节点更先进，以及芯片含量不断增加（M系列 Mac、AirPods、Apple Watch）。苹果或许正在流失台积电总产量的份额，但其从单台设备中攫取的价值却创下历史新高。

paragraphs.source: Apple is not standing still. The 1.4nm ramp in 2029 shows Apple reclaiming 67% node share, suggesting strategic capacity reservations made years in advance. Rumors of Baltra, Apple’s server-class silicon for internal AI infrastructure, point to a potential expansion into HPC territory. And revenue per smartphone has tripled since 2012 as die sizes grow and silicon content expands across the product portfolio. Apple may lose relative share of TSMC’s output, but it is extracting more value per device than ever before. The question is whether that is enough. When AI wafer demand is growing at 2x Apple’s rate and hyperscalers are writing multi-billion dollar prepayment checks, even the world’s most important smartphone customer finds itself competing for attention at the foundry.

And revenue per smartphone has tripled since 2012 as die sizes grow and silicon content expands across the product portfolio. Apple may lose relative share of TSMC’s output, but it is extracting more value per device than ever before. The question is whether that is enough. When AI wafer demand is growing at 2x Apple’s rate and hyperscalers are writing multi-billion dollar prepayment checks, even the world’s most important smartphone customer finds itself competing for attention at the foundry. The AI wave is too big to ignore, and Apple’s position at TSMC, while still formidable, is no longer uncontested.

The AI wave is too big to ignore, and Apple’s position at TSMC, while still formidable, is no longer uncontested.

pro.text: 苹果并未坐以待毙。2029 年的 1.4nm 产能爬坡显示，苹果将重新夺回 67% 的节点份额，这表明其提前数年就进行了战略性产能预订。有关 Baltra（苹果用于内部 AI 基础设施的服务器级芯片）的传闻，预示着其可能向 HPC 领域扩张。随着裸片尺寸增大以及整个产品组合中芯片含量的提升，单部智能手机营收自 2012 年以来已增至三倍。苹果或许会流失台积电产量的相对份额，但其从单台设备中攫取的价值却创下历史新高。问题在于，这是否足够。当 AI 晶圆需求以两倍于苹果的速度增长，且超大规模云厂商纷纷开出数十亿美元的预付款支票时，即便是全球最重要的智能手机客户，也不得不在晶圆代工厂里争夺关注度。

paragraphs.source: In 2022, HPC revenue surpassed smartphones at TSMC for the first time, and the gap has widened every quarter since. By 2025, HPC is projected to exceed $71 billion, more than double the $35 billion from smartphones. The driver is obvious: AI accelerators from NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscaler custom silicon are absorbing capacity at a pace smartphones never did.

pro.text: 2022 年，台积电的 HPC 营收首次超越智能手机，此后每个季度这一差距都在不断拉大。预计到 2025 年，HPC 营收将突破 710 亿美元，达到智能手机营收（350 亿美元）的两倍以上。驱动因素显而易见：来自英伟达、AMD 的 AI 加速器以及超大规模云服务商的定制芯片，正以智能手机从未有过的速度吞噬产能。

paragraphs.source: NVIDIA alone now rivals Apple in leading-edge wafer consumption, and the broader AI ecosystem is growing at nearly 22% CAGR versus 11% for smartphones. Apple’s share of TSMC’s total revenue fell from 24% in 2023 to 17% in 2025, even as Apple’s absolute spend continued rising. For the first time, Apple is being outbid for capacity.

paragraphs.source: One of the most misunderstood aspects of the Apple-TSMC relationship is the pricing model. Surface-level analysis assumes a fixed wafer price. The reality is a dynamic risk-sharing agreement that evolves over the lifecycle of a process node.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: One of the most misunderstood aspects of the Apple-TSMC relationship is the pricing model. Surface-level analysis assumes a fixed wafer price. The reality is a dynamic risk-sharing agreement that evolves over the lifecycle of a process node.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: One of the most misunderstood aspects of the Apple-TSMC relationship is the pricing model. Surface-level analysis assumes a fixed wafer price. The reality is a dynamic risk-sharing agreement that evolves over the lifecycle of a process node.

pro.text: 苹果与台积电的合作关系中，最容易被误解的一点便是定价模型。浅层分析往往假定晶圆价格固定不变。实际情况却是一项动态的风险分担协议，该协议会随着制程节点生命周期的推进而不断演变。

paragraphs.source: When TSMC launches a bleeding-edge node (e.g., N3 for the A17 Pro), yields can be as low as 55% during the initial ramp. A standard wafer pricing model ($20,000/wafer) would be ruinous for Apple if nearly half the chips on each wafer are defective.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: When TSMC launches a bleeding-edge node (e.g., N3 for the A17 Pro), yields can be as low as 55% during the initial ramp. A standard wafer pricing model ($20,000/wafer) would be ruinous for Apple if nearly half the chips on each wafer are defective.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: When TSMC launches a bleeding-edge node (e.g., N3 for the A17 Pro), yields can be as low as 55% during the initial ramp. A standard wafer pricing model ($20,000/wafer) would be ruinous for Apple if nearly half the chips on each wafer are defective.

pro.text: 当台积电推出最尖端的制程节点（例如用于 A17 Pro 的 N3）时，在初始产能爬坡阶段，良率可能低至 55%。如果每片晶圆上近半数芯片都是次品，采用标准的晶圆定价模式（每片晶圆 20,000 美元）对苹果来说将是毁灭性的。

paragraphs.source: The Deal: Apple negotiates to pay only for Known Good Dies (KGD) during the initial ramp. TSMC absorbs the cost of the defects. This effectively aligns incentives: TSMC must improve yield to make money, and Apple is protected from the immaturity of the node.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The Deal: Apple negotiates to pay only for Known Good Dies (KGD) during the initial ramp. TSMC absorbs the cost of the defects. This effectively aligns incentives: TSMC must improve yield to make money, and Apple is protected from the immaturity of the node.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The Deal: Apple negotiates to pay only for Known Good Dies (KGD) during the initial ramp. TSMC absorbs the cost of the defects. This effectively aligns incentives: TSMC must improve yield to make money, and Apple is protected from the immaturity of the node.

pro.text: 交易方案：苹果通过谈判达成协议，在初始产能爬坡期间仅为已知良好裸片（KGD）买单。台积电则承担次品成本。这有效统一了双方的利益诉求：台积电必须提高良率才能盈利，而苹果也免受制程节点不成熟的拖累。

paragraphs.source: The Trade-off: Apple commits to enormous volumes (often buying 50-100% of initial capacity) that no other customer can match, effectively funding the R&D and depreciation of the fab.

paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The Trade-off: Apple commits to enormous volumes (often buying 50-100% of initial capacity) that no other customer can match, effectively funding the R&D and depreciation of the fab.

pro.text: 利益交换：苹果承诺提供其他客户无法企及的庞大订单量（通常买断 50% 到 100% 的初始产能），这实际上为晶圆厂的研发和折旧提供了资金支持。

paragraphs.source: A-Series: Less binning flexibility (iPhone volumes require uniformity), though the A18 vs A18 Pro distinction (5-core vs 6-core GPU) suggests aggressive binning is now entering the iPhone lineup to combat N3E yield costs. This allows Apple to use dies with a single GPU defect in the standard iPhone 16.

pro.text: A系列：芯片分级灵活性较低（iPhone 的庞大出货量要求芯片具备高度一致性）。不过，A18 与 A18 Pro 的差异（5 核与 6 核 GPU）表明，激进的芯片分级策略正引入 iPhone 产品线，以对冲 N3E 的良率成本。这使得苹果能在标准版 iPhone 16 中使用存在单个 GPU 缺陷的裸片。

paragraphs.source: TSMC’s capex trajectory reveals three distinct eras. Pre-Apple (2005-2009), annual spending averaged $2.4B. The foundry had no anchor tenant and limited visibility into future demand. Post-Apple (2010-2018), capex climbed to $8B annually as A-series volumes justified larger bets. The EUV era (2019-2022) broke the model entirely. TSMC deployed $98B in four years, exceeding the cumulative $92B spent over the prior 14 years. This acceleration required certainty. Apple’s volume commitments and manufacturing purchase obligations gave TSMC the confidence to make these bets. Without a guaranteed buyer for bleeding-edge nodes, no foundry can justify $30B+ annual capex.

pro.text: 台积电的资本支出轨迹呈现出三个截然不同的时代。前苹果时代（2005-2009 年），年均支出仅 24 亿美元。当时这家晶圆代工厂缺乏锚定客户，对未来需求的可见度也十分有限。后苹果时代（2010-2018 年），A系列芯片的庞大出货量为更大规模的投资提供了依据，资本支出攀升至每年 80 亿美元。EUV 时代（2019-2022 年）彻底打破了这一模式。台积电在四年内狂砸 980 亿美元，超过了此前 14 年累计支出的 920 亿美元。这种加速扩张需要极高的确定性。正是苹果的产量承诺与制造采购承诺，赋予了台积电重金下注的底气。如果尖端制程节点没有保底买家，任何晶圆代工厂都无法支撑起每年超 300 亿美元的资本支出。

paragraphs.source: Apple has effectively financed $73B of TSMC’s leading-edge capacity since the A8 launched on 20nm in 2014, representing 47% of all advanced node capex over that period. This is 1.7x the $43B implied by Apple’s 22-25% revenue share. The premium stems from Apple’s role as sole anchor tenant at node launch: near 100% of 20nm in 2014 and 10nm in 2017, and over 90% of N3 in 2023.

This is 1.7x the $43B implied by Apple’s 22-25% revenue share. The premium stems from Apple’s role as sole anchor tenant at node launch: near 100% of 20nm in 2014 and 10nm in 2017, and over 90% of N3 in 2023.

The premium stems from Apple’s role as sole anchor tenant at node launch: near 100% of 20nm in 2014 and 10nm in 2017, and over 90% of N3 in 2023.

pro.text: 自 2014 年 A8 芯片在 20 纳米制程节点发布以来，苹果实质上为台积电 730 亿美元的先进制程产能提供了资金支持，占同期所有先进制程节点资本支出的 47%。按照苹果 22% 至 25% 的营收占比推算，其对应的资金规模应为 430 亿美元，而实际出资额是这一数字的 1.7 倍。这种溢价源于苹果在节点发布初期的独家锚定客户地位：2014 年的 20 纳米和 2017 年的 10 纳米节点，苹果几乎包揽了 100% 的产能；2023 年的 N3 节点，苹果也吃下了超 90% 的产能。

paragraphs.source: These figures use lifetime utilization weighting, crediting Apple only for its share of each node’s output over a 5-year depreciation cycle rather than assigning full launch-year capex. Even under this conservative methodology, Apple’s contribution dwarfs all other customers combined. TSMC’s cumulative $259B capex through 2025 translates to $83B in Apple-attributed infrastructure, exceeding Apple’s own semiconductor R&D spend over the same period.

pro.text: 上述数据采用了全生命周期利用率加权算法。该算法并未将节点发布首年的资本支出全额记在苹果账上，而是按 5 年折旧周期内苹果在该节点总产量中的份额进行折算。即使采用这种保守的计算方法，苹果的贡献依然令所有其他客户的总和相形见绌。截至 2025 年，台积电累计 2590 亿美元的资本支出中，有 830 亿美元的基础设施投资可归功于苹果。这一数字甚至超过了苹果同期自身的半导体研发支出。

paragraphs.source: Qualcomm, AMD, NVIDIA, and MediaTek collectively account for 25% of TSMC revenue but bear roughly 30% of leading-edge capex burden because they backfill capacity 12-18 months after Apple has absorbed yield risk and funded the initial ramp. The dynamic creates mutual lock-in: Apple gets first access to density and efficiency gains, TSMC gets a guaranteed anchor tenant to de-risk $25-30B annual node transitions.

The dynamic creates mutual lock-in: Apple gets first access to density and efficiency gains, TSMC gets a guaranteed anchor tenant to de-risk $25-30B annual node transitions.

pro.text: 高通、AMD、英伟达和联发科合计贡献了台积电 25% 的营收，却承担了约 30% 的先进制程资本支出重担。这是因为它们通常在苹果消化了良率风险并为初期产能爬坡提供资金的 12 到 18 个月后，才来填补产能空缺。这种动态机制促成了双向绑定：苹果得以率先享受晶体管密度和效率提升的红利；台积电则获得了一个有保障的锚定客户，从而化解每年高达 250 亿至 300 亿美元制程节点迭代的巨额风险。

paragraphs.source: TSMC’s R&D spending has grown 8x since 2010, from under $1B to $8B in 2025. But R&D intensity tells a different story. In the 2015-2019 period when Apple was the primary driver of leading-edge scaling, TSMC consistently spent 8%+ of revenue on R&D. That ratio has since dropped to under 7%, not because TSMC is investing less, but because AI-driven revenue is scaling faster than the R&D required to support it. Apple funded the hard yards of EUV transition and sub-7nm development; AI customers are now harvesting the returns on that investment at scale.

pro.text: 自 2010 年以来，台积电的研发支出增长了 8 倍，从不足 10 亿美元攀升至 2025 年的 80 亿美元。但研发强度却呈现出另一番景象。在 2015 至 2019 年间，当苹果还是推动先进制程演进的主力时，台积电的研发支出始终保持在营收的 8% 以上。如今该比例已降至 7% 以下，这并非因为台积电缩减了投资，而是因为 AI 驱动的营收增速，远超支撑其所需的研发投入增速。苹果出资扛过了 EUV 转型和 7 纳米以下制程开发的攻坚阶段；而 AI 客户如今正在大规模收割这些投资的红利。

paragraphs.source: TSMC’s customer concentration has steadily increased over the past two decades, with the top-10 customers growing from 44% of revenue in 2000 to 78% by 2025. Apple has been a primary driver of this concentration. Before 2014, Apple was a negligible contributor to TSMC’s revenue; by 2025, Apple alone represents roughly a quarter of top-10 customer revenue. For most of the 2014-2022 period, Apple grew faster than the average of the other nine top-10 customers, pulling TSMC’s customer mix increasingly toward Cupertino.

For most of the 2014-2022 period, Apple grew faster than the average of the other nine top-10 customers, pulling TSMC’s customer mix increasingly toward Cupertino.

pro.text: 过去二十年间，台积电的客户集中度稳步攀升，前十大客户的营收占比从 2000 年的 44% 增长至 2025 年的 78%。苹果一直是推动这一集中度上升的核心动力。2014 年之前，苹果对台积电的营收贡献微乎其微；而到 2025 年，仅苹果一家就占据了前十大客户营收的约四分之一。在 2014 至 2022 年的大部分时间里，苹果的增速超过了前十大客户中其余九家的平均水平，使得台积电的客户结构日益向库比蒂诺（译注：苹果总部）倾斜。

paragraphs.source: That dynamic has now reversed. In 2024 and 2025, non-Apple revenue at TSMC grew at nearly double Apple’s rate, driven by AI accelerator demand from NVIDIA, Broadcom, AMD, and hyperscaler custom silicon. The ex-Apple top-10 cohort, which grew at single-digit rates for much of the 2010s, is now expanding at 40%+ annually. Apple remains TSMC’s largest single customer, but its growth is being outpaced by a coalition of AI-focused buyers who are collectively reshaping the foundry’s revenue base. The customer concentration continues to rise, but the center of gravity is shifting away from smartphones and toward data center silicon.

pro.text: 如今，这种动态已经逆转。在 2024 和 2025 年，受英伟达、博通、AMD 的 AI 加速器以及超大规模云服务商定制芯片的需求驱动，台积电非苹果业务的营收增速几乎是苹果的两倍。在 2010 年代的大部分时间里，除苹果外的前十大客户群体的增速仅为个位数，而如今正以每年 40% 以上的速度扩张。苹果依然是台积电最大的单一客户，但其增速已被一个由 AI 核心买家组成的联盟所超越，这些买家正在合力重塑这家晶圆代工厂的营收基本盘。客户集中度仍在持续攀升，但重心正从智能手机向数据中心芯片转移。

paragraphs.source: Apple pioneered TSMC’s advanced packaging business. InFO launched with the A10 in 2016, and Apple scaled it from $1.8B in 2018 to $3.8B in 2025. CoWoS was a rounding error at $118M in 2018. By 2025, it reached $9.6B. The crossover happened in 2022 when AI demand exploded. Apple created TSMC’s advanced packaging business with InFO. Nvidia’s CoWoS demand now drives it.

CoWoS was a rounding error at $118M in 2018. By 2025, it reached $9.6B. The crossover happened in 2022 when AI demand exploded. Apple created TSMC’s advanced packaging business with InFO. Nvidia’s CoWoS demand now drives it.

Nvidia’s CoWoS demand now drives it.

pro.text: 苹果开创了台积电的先进封装业务。InFO 于 2016 年随 A10 首次亮相，苹果将其规模从 2018 年的 18 亿美元一路推升至 2025 年的 38 亿美元。2018 年，CoWoS 营收仅为 1.18 亿美元，堪称零头；到 2025 年，这一数字将高达 96 亿美元。转折点发生在 2022 年，当时 AI 需求迎来爆发。苹果凭借 InFO 一手缔造了台积电的先进封装业务，而如今，英伟达对 CoWoS 的需求已成为核心驱动力。

issues.description: 原文的“crossover”在这里指代图表上两条增长曲线（InFO 和 CoWoS 营收）的交叉，即 CoWoS 营收反超 InFO 营收的时间点。译为“转折点”虽然通顺，但丢失了“反超/交叉”的数学与视觉含义。
“绝对封装营收”略带翻译腔，原文的“absolute”是为了与前文的“share”（份额/相对值）作对比。
中英文字符之间缺少空格。

paragraphs.source: Apple accounted for 78% of TSMC’s non-wafer revenue in 2020. That was peak dominance. InFO was scaling for A-series chips, and CoWoS had few customers. By 2024, Apple’s share dropped to 36%. By 2025, it falls further to 30%. Apple’s absolute packaging revenue continues growing. But AI customers, led by Nvidia, are scaling CoWoS faster.

By 2024, Apple’s share dropped to 36%. By 2025, it falls further to 30%. Apple’s absolute packaging revenue continues growing. But AI customers, led by Nvidia, are scaling CoWoS faster.

pro.text: 2020 年，苹果占据了台积电非晶圆营收的 78%，这是其统治力的巅峰。当时 InFO 正为 A系列芯片扩大规模，而 CoWoS 客户寥寥无几。到 2024 年，苹果的份额跌至 36%；到 2025 年，这一数字将进一步降至 30%。苹果的绝对封装营收仍在持续增长，但以英伟达为首的 AI 客户，正以更快的速度扩张 CoWoS 规模。

paragraphs.source: Prior to 2020, Apple paid Intel approximately $250 for a possibly overheating laptop CPU. Today, the M-series chip costs Apple estimated $40-50 to manufacture.

paragraphs.source: The Mac gross margin story is the clearest proof of Apple’s silicon thesis. In 2019, Mac GM sat at 28.5%, constrained by Intel’s $150+ processor ASPs and a roadmap that delivered clock speed regression instead of progress. By 2025, Mac GM stands at 39.5%. That 11 percentage point expansion on a $40B Mac business translates to roughly $4.4 billion in incremental annual gross profit.

In 2019, Mac GM sat at 28.5%, constrained by Intel’s $150+ processor ASPs and a roadmap that delivered clock speed regression instead of progress. By 2025, Mac GM stands at 39.5%. That 11 percentage point expansion on a $40B Mac business translates to roughly $4.4 billion in incremental annual gross profit.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The Mac gross margin story is the clearest proof of Apple’s silicon thesis. In 2019, Mac GM sat at 28.5%, constrained by Intel’s $150+ processor ASPs and a roadmap that delivered clock speed regression instead of progress. By 2025, Mac GM stands at 39.5%. That 11 percentage point expansion on a $40B Mac business translates to roughly $4.4 billion in incremental annual gross profit.

pro.text: Mac 产品线的毛利率表现，是苹果自研芯片战略最强有力的佐证。2019 年，受制于英特尔高达 150 美元以上的处理器平均售价 (ASP)，外加其产品路线图上的时钟频率不进反退，Mac 毛利率仅徘徊在 28.5%。到 2025 年，Mac 毛利率已攀升至 39.5%。在规模达 400 亿美元的 Mac 业务中，这 11 个百分点的增幅，意味着每年约 44 亿美元的新增毛利润。

paragraphs.source: Intel’s failure to ship 10nm at competitive yields for over five years represented a multi-billion dollar transfer of value from Cupertino to Santa Clara. Apple finally recaptured it. The M-series chips cost Apple an estimated $77 per unit in COGS (N3 wafers, packaging, yield loss) versus Intel’s $150+ Core i9 ASP. At 65 million Mac units annually, the transition generates $4.77 billion in gross profit lift. Break-even is 14 million units against a $1 billion NRE investment.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Intel’s failure to ship 10nm at competitive yields for over five years represented a multi-billion dollar transfer of value from Cupertino to Santa Clara. Apple finally recaptured it. The M-series chips cost Apple an estimated $77 per unit in COGS (N3 wafers, packaging, yield loss) versus Intel’s $150+ Core i9 ASP. At 65 million Mac units annually, the transition generates $4.77 billion in gross profit lift. Break-even is 14 million units against a $1 billion NRE investment.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Intel’s failure to ship 10nm at competitive yields for over five years represented a multi-billion dollar transfer of value from Cupertino to Santa Clara. Apple finally recaptured it. The M-series chips cost Apple an estimated $77 per unit in COGS (N3 wafers, packaging, yield loss) versus Intel’s $150+ Core i9 ASP. At 65 million Mac units annually, the transition generates $4.77 billion in gross profit lift. Break-even is 14 million units against a $1 billion NRE investment.

pro.text: 英特尔长达五年多未能以具备竞争力的良率量产 10 纳米工艺，导致数十亿美元的价值从苹果流向了英特尔。如今，苹果终于夺回了这笔利润。M系列芯片的单颗销货成本 (COGS)（涵盖 N3 晶圆、封装及良率损耗）预估为 77 美元，相比之下，英特尔 Core i9 的平均售价 (ASP) 则高达 150 美元以上。按每年 6500 万台 Mac 的销量计算，此次架构转换可带来 47.7 亿美元的毛利润提升。面对 10 亿美元的一次性工程费用 (NRE) 投入，其盈亏平衡点仅为 1400 万颗。

paragraphs.source: The ongoing transition away from Qualcomm (estimated $1.2B/year savings) represents the final major piece of the puzzle.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The ongoing transition away from Qualcomm (estimated $1.2B/year savings) represents the final major piece of the puzzle.

pro.text: 逐步摆脱对高通的依赖（预计每年节省 12 亿美元），是苹果自研芯片版图的最后一块关键拼图。

paragraphs.source: The C1 modem represents Apple’s most expensive and most contentious insourcing bet. Qualcomm’s X75 5G chip commands a $20+ ASP with gross margins of 50%. Apple’s C1 alternative, built on N4 with a 50mm² die, costs an estimated $14.40 in COGS. The $5 per-unit savings across 250 million cellular iOS devices yields $1.2B in annual gross profit improvement.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The C1 modem represents Apple’s most expensive and most contentious insourcing bet. Qualcomm’s X75 5G chip commands a $20+ ASP with gross margins of 50%. Apple’s C1 alternative, built on N4 with a 50mm² die, costs an estimated $14.40 in COGS. The $5 per-unit savings across 250 million cellular iOS devices yields $1.2B in annual gross profit improvement.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The C1 modem represents Apple’s most expensive and most contentious insourcing bet. Qualcomm’s X75 5G chip commands a $20+ ASP with gross margins of 50%. Apple’s C1 alternative, built on N4 with a 50mm² die, costs an estimated $14.40 in COGS. The $5 per-unit savings across 250 million cellular iOS devices yields $1.2B in annual gross profit improvement.

pro.text: C1基带是苹果代价最高、也最具争议的一场自研豪赌。高通的 X75 5G 芯片平均售价 (ASP) 超过 20 美元，毛利率高达 50%。相比之下，苹果自研的 C1基带采用 N4 工艺，裸片面积为 50 平方毫米，单颗销货成本 (COGS) 估计为 14.40 美元。在 2.5 亿台支持蜂窝网络的 iOS 设备中，单机节省的 5 美元每年可带来 12 亿美元的毛利提升。

paragraphs.source: The real story is the NRE. Apple spent $6B (including the $1B Intel modem acquisition, 2,200 engineers, mask sets, and qualification costs) to get here. Break-even volume is 1.3B units, roughly 5 years of iPhone production. By 2027, when Qualcomm is fully phased out, the recurring savings become pure margin expansion.

Break-even volume is 1.3B units, roughly 5 years of iPhone production. By 2027, when Qualcomm is fully phased out, the recurring savings become pure margin expansion.

Break-even volume is 1.3B units, roughly 5 years of iPhone production. By 2027, when Qualcomm is fully phased out, the recurring savings become pure margin expansion.

pro.text: 真正的重头戏在于一次性工程费用 (NRE)。为了达成这一目标，苹果砸下了 60 亿美元（包括斥资 10 亿美元收购英特尔基带业务、组建 2200 人的工程师团队，以及光罩套件和认证成本）。该项目的盈亏平衡销量高达 13 亿颗，大约相当于 iPhone 五年的总产量。到 2027 年高通芯片被彻底淘汰时，这些持续节省下来的成本将转化为纯粹的利润率扩张。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s move against Broadcom in Wi-Fi and Bluetooth receives less attention but carries equal significance. The N1 chip, Apple’s first in-house Wi-Fi 7 solution, replaces Broadcom’s $11 combo chip with an internally designed alternative costing $8.7 in COGS. At $2.3 savings per unit across 300 million iOS devices, that’s $700M in annual gross profit recapture. The NRE was relatively modest at $250M, yielding break-even at 110 million units. Breakeven likely within the first year of production.

pro.text: 苹果在 Wi-Fi 和蓝牙领域向博通发起的攻势鲜受关注，但意义同样重大。N1 是苹果首款自研 Wi-Fi 7 解决方案，它取代了博通售价 11 美元的组合芯片，其单颗 COGS 降至 8.7 美元。在 3 亿台 iOS 设备中，单机节省 2.3 美元，每年可夺回 7 亿美元的毛利。该项目的 NRE 相对较低，仅为 2.5 亿美元，盈亏平衡销量为 1.1 亿颗。预计在量产的首年内即可实现盈亏平衡。

paragraphs.source: These savings recur and compound annually. Each A-series generation inherits prior design wins. Each M-series chip amortizes NRE across Mac, iPad, and now Vision Pro. The $7.5B in combined annual savings (Intel CPU: $5B, Qualcomm Modem: $1.2B, Broadcom Connectivity: $700M, GPU/NPU IP: $0.5B, plus efficiency gains) funds the next round of R&D. Apple plows savings back into R&D. Android OEMs still pay Qualcomm and MediaTek for chips Apple now makes itself.

Apple plows savings back into R&D. Android OEMs still pay Qualcomm and MediaTek for chips Apple now makes itself.

pro.text: 这些节约下来的成本每年都会循环产生并带来复利效应。每一代 A系列芯片都能继承前代的设计成果。每一款 M系列芯片都能在 Mac、iPad 以及如今的 Vision Pro 产品线中摊销 NRE。每年合计高达 75 亿美元的成本节约（英特尔 CPU：50 亿美元；高通基带：12 亿美元；博通连接芯片：7 亿美元；GPU/NPU IP：5 亿美元，外加效率提升带来的收益）为下一轮研发提供了资金弹药。苹果将节约的资金反哺研发，而安卓手机厂商却仍在向高通和联发科购买苹果如今已能自研的芯片。

paragraphs.source: Huawei, Samsung, Xiaomi, ZTE, Google and others tried to replicate Apple’s success in vertical integration but failed to put together a coherent strategy. Huawei seemed like a challenger but got cut off at TSMC due to export controls. Huawei is nearly sufficient and has invested in much wider areas than Apple, including deep into supply chain layers such as lithography, fabs and memory. But its success is slowed by China’s limited capacity in advanced nodes.

paragraphs.source: For Apple, the return on invested capital (ROIC) of building a fab is negative. Using TSMC’s balance sheet as a service allows Apple to maintain 35-40% corporate operating margins. Owning a fab would dilute those margins significantly while introducing operational risks (yield crashes, node delays) that Apple currently outsources.

Owning a fab would dilute those margins significantly while introducing operational risks (yield crashes, node delays) that Apple currently outsources.

pro.text: 对苹果而言，自建晶圆厂的投资资本回报率（ROIC）为负。将台积电的资产负债表作为一种服务（BaaS）来使用，让苹果得以维持35%至40%的公司营业利润率。自持晶圆厂将大幅稀释这一利润率，并引入良率崩溃、节点延迟等运营风险，而苹果目前已将这些风险转嫁给代工厂。

paragraphs.source: Arizona wafer costs run 2.4x higher than Taiwan’s Fab 18. Labor is 3x more expensive. Foundation construction costs 4x as much. Raw materials run 2x. Utilities are the only win: water costs half as much, electricity 50% cheaper. These savings are dwarfed by the labor and construction delta.

pro.text: 亚利桑那州的晶圆成本是台湾地区 Fab 18 的 2.4 倍。劳动力成本是其 3 倍。地基建设成本是其 4 倍。原材料成本翻倍。水电等公用事业是唯一的成本洼地：水费便宜一半，电费低 50%。然而，在劳动力与建设成本的巨大鸿沟面前，这点节约可谓杯水车薪。

issues.description: 中文语境中，“高出 2.4 倍”通常在数学上意味着 1+2.4=3.4 倍，这会夸大原文的成本差距。结合第3段的“At 2.4x the cost”可知，这里的 2.4x 和 3x 指的是成本的绝对倍数，而非增量倍数。应统一改为“是……的 X 倍”。
译者遵照了提示中的建议添加了括注，但“（这里的 A14 指台积电的 1.4 纳米节点，而非苹果处理器）”作为句内插入语略显冗长，打断了行文的专业节奏。按照 Guideline 1，可以直接将节点名称显性化，既消除歧义又保持简洁。

paragraphs.source: Apple is already producing chips at Fab 21. TSMC lists Apple, AMD, Qualcomm, and NVIDIA as Phase 1 customers. Apple’s mobile SoCs, around 100mm² die size, are among the early production volume. Yields reportedly exceeded Taiwan by four percentage points at the same ramp stage, though the product mix skews toward smaller dies rather than Nvidia’s 800mm² GPUs. The fab reached 15K wafers per month in 2Q25 and is ramping toward 24K wspm.

The fab reached 15K wafers per month in 2Q25 and is ramping toward 24K wspm.

pro.text: 苹果已在 Fab 21 投产芯片。台积电将苹果、AMD、高通与英伟达列为一期客户。裸片尺寸约 100 平方毫米的苹果移动 SoC 是早期量产的主力。据报道，在同等产能爬坡阶段，其良率较台湾地区高出 4 个百分点；但需注意，该厂的产品组合主要向小尺寸裸片倾斜，而非英伟达 800 平方毫米的巨型 GPU。2025 年第二季度，该晶圆厂的每月晶圆投片量已达 1.5 万片，并正向 2.4 万片的目标持续爬坡。

paragraphs.source: The node lag matters more than cost. Taiwan’s “N-2 principle” requires overseas fabs to trail domestic production by at least two generations. If TSMC is developing 1.2nm or 1.4nm in Taiwan, only 1.6nm can go abroad. Process technologies at 14nm and below are classified as critical technologies, covering equipment and materials. TSMC’s 10,000+ R&D engineers remain in Hsinchu. The “mother fab” for each new node must be in Taiwan. By the time Arizona receives N2, Taiwan will be running A14 or beyond. Fab 21 represents less than 15% of TSMC’s total N5/N3 capacity. Arizona hedges Taiwan risk. It does not eliminate Taiwan dependency.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The node lag matters more than cost. Taiwan’s “N-2 principle” requires overseas fabs to trail domestic production by at least two generations. If TSMC is developing 1.2nm or 1.4nm in Taiwan, only 1.6nm can go abroad. Process technologies at 14nm and below are classified as critical technologies, covering equipment and materials. TSMC’s 10,000+ R&D engineers remain in Hsinchu. The “mother fab” for each new node must be in Taiwan. By the time Arizona receives N2, Taiwan will be running A14 or beyond. Fab 21 represents less than 15% of TSMC’s total N5/N3 capacity. Arizona hedges Taiwan risk. It does not eliminate Taiwan dependency.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The node lag matters more than cost. Taiwan’s “N-2 principle” requires overseas fabs to trail domestic production by at least two generations. If TSMC is developing 1.2nm or 1.4nm in Taiwan, only 1.6nm can go abroad. Process technologies at 14nm and below are classified as critical technologies, covering equipment and materials. TSMC’s 10,000+ R&D engineers remain in Hsinchu. The “mother fab” for each new node must be in Taiwan. By the time Arizona receives N2, Taiwan will be running A14 or beyond. Fab 21 represents less than 15% of TSMC’s total N5/N3 capacity. Arizona hedges Taiwan risk. It does not eliminate Taiwan dependency.

pro.text: 制程代差的影响远甚于成本。台湾地区的“N-2”原则要求海外晶圆厂的技术代际必须落后本土至少两代。若台积电在台湾地区研发 1.2 纳米或 1.4 纳米，海外最多只能引入 1.6 纳米。14 纳米及以下工艺技术（涵盖设备与材料）均被列为关键技术。台积电逾万名研发工程师仍留守新竹。每个新制程节点的母厂必须设在台湾地区。当亚利桑那州工厂终于等来 N2 时，台湾地区早已量产台积电 A14（1.4纳米）甚至更先进制程。Fab 21 在台积电 N5/N3 总产能中的占比不足 15%。亚利桑那州工厂仅能对冲台湾地区的风险，无法消除对台湾地区的依赖。

paragraphs.source: Apple can absorb higher costs as a hedge against Taiwan risk. But Apple won’t shift majority production to Arizona unless forced. At 2.4x the cost of Taiwan wafers, the economics don’t justify it absent geopolitical catastrophe.

pro.text: 苹果有能力消化溢价，借此对冲台湾地区的地缘风险。但除非迫不得已，苹果绝不会将主力产能迁至亚利桑那州。面对高达台湾地区 2.4 倍的晶圆成本，若非爆发地缘政治灾难，这种转移在经济账上根本站不住脚。

pro.text: 目前，仅有 5% 的先进制程产能来自亚利桑那州。若到 2028 年该比例攀升至 10% 至 15%，则明确释放出信号：苹果对台湾地区局势深感忧虑。若比例停滞在 5%，那么亚利桑那州工厂充其量只是一份纯粹的保单。

paragraphs.source: TSMC’s A16 (N2) is architected for HPC workloads. Backside power delivery, gate-all-around transistors, and thermal envelopes are optimized for data center power budgets. Nvidia benefits disproportionately. Apple will adopt A16 for the A19/M5 generation, but the node’s design tradeoffs favor Nvidia’s use case over Apple’s. This is why Apple’s share drops to <50% on N2.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: TSMC’s A16 (N2) is architected for HPC workloads. Backside power delivery, gate-all-around transistors, and thermal envelopes are optimized for data center power budgets. Nvidia benefits disproportionately. Apple will adopt A16 for the A19/M5 generation, but the node’s design tradeoffs favor Nvidia’s use case over Apple’s. This is why Apple’s share drops to <50% on N2.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: TSMC’s A16 (N2) is architected for HPC workloads. Backside power delivery, gate-all-around transistors, and thermal envelopes are optimized for data center power budgets. Nvidia benefits disproportionately. Apple will adopt A16 for the A19/M5 generation, but the node’s design tradeoffs favor Nvidia’s use case over Apple’s. This is why Apple’s share drops to <50% on N2.

pro.text: 台积电 A16（N2）节点专为 HPC 工作负载而设计。背面供电、环绕栅极晶体管以及散热设计均针对数据中心的功耗预算进行了优化。英伟达从中获益尤丰。苹果将在 A19/M5 代芯片上采用 A16 节点，但该节点的设计权衡更偏向英伟达的应用场景，而非苹果。这正是苹果在 N2 节点上的份额跌破 50% 的原因。

issues.description: 译文直接使用了“A16”和“A14”，未严格遵循翻译指南中关于“explicitly translate as '台积电A16节点'”的要求。在苹果和台积电的上下文中，脱离“节点”二字单独出现 A16 和 A14，极易与苹果的 A 系列手机芯片（A16 Bionic、A14 Bionic）混淆。
原文的“AI mega datacenters”在此处是借代用法，指代的是这些数据中心背后的运营方（如超大规模云厂商），直接译为“与数据中心交流”在中文搭配上略显生硬。

paragraphs.source: A14 (1.4nm) rebalances the equation. TSMC is designing A14 for both mobile and HPC from the start. Power efficiency curves favor consumer devices again. Our model shows Apple reclaims 67% node share on A14, its highest since N3. The timing aligns with Apple’s product cadence: A21 Pro and M7 in 2029-2030.

pro.text: A14（1.4纳米）节点重新平衡了这一格局。台积电从一开始就将 A14 定位于同时满足移动端和 HPC 需求。能效曲线再次向消费级设备倾斜。我们的模型显示，苹果将在 A14 上收复 67% 的节点份额，创下自 N3 以来的新高。这一时间点与苹果的产品发布节奏完美契合：即 2029 至 2030 年推出的 A21 Pro 与 M7。

paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: We will also be spending time with telecom companies and equipment vendors to dive deep into exciting developments in scale-across networking.

pro.text: 我们也将与电信公司及设备供应商展开交流，深入探讨跨集群扩展网络领域令人振奋的新进展。

issues.description: 译文直接使用了“A16”和“A14”，未严格遵循翻译指南中关于“explicitly translate as '台积电A16节点'”的要求。在苹果和台积电的上下文中，脱离“节点”二字单独出现 A16 和 A14，极易与苹果的 A 系列手机芯片（A16 Bionic、A14 Bionic）混淆。
原文的“AI mega datacenters”在此处是借代用法，指代的是这些数据中心背后的运营方（如超大规模云厂商），直接译为“与数据中心交流”在中文搭配上略显生硬。

paragraphs.source: Join us at our Happy Hour hosted on Sunday January 18! We'd love to connect with you.
